{{Otheruses|Kido River in the Kujūkuri region|the tributary of the Tonegawa system in Funabashi City|Kido River (Funabashi)}} {{Infobox river | name = Kido River | image = [[File:Kidoriver2.jpg|300px]] | image_caption = View downstream from Kido Bridge | watercourse = [[Secondary watercourse (Japan)|Secondary watercourse]] | watercourse_system = Kido River system | type = [[Secondary river]] | length = 21.5 km | elevation = 35 m | discharge = | observation_station = | basin_area = 72.1 km² | source = [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]], Honjō area | mouth = [[Pacific Ocean]] (Sanmu City) | basin = [[Sanmu, Chiba|Sanmu City]], [[Sanbu District, Chiba|Sanbu District]] (Shibayama Town, Yokoshibahikari Town), [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]] | footnotes = | source_url = [https://www.maff.go.jp/kanto/nouson/sekkei/kokuei/ryoso/shizen/01_2.html] }} [[File:Kidoriver1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Upstream view from Kido Bridge]] '''Kido River''' (木戸川, *Kido-gawa*) is a [[secondary river]] flowing through the '''central Kujūkuri Plain''' in '''northeastern Chiba Prefecture, Japan'''. It is the main river of the '''Kido River system'''.
== Geography == The Kido River originates in '''Narita City’s Honjō area''', where it is fed by an '''adjustment pond''', before flowing '''southeast'''. It runs along the '''border of Shibayama Town''' in Sanbu District and then '''passes through the western part of the town''', where it is joined by '''several small tributaries''' from the west.
Exiting Shibayama, the river marks the '''boundary between Yokoshibahikari Town and Sanmu City''' before entering '''Sanmu City'''. Along its course, '''historical sites''' such as the '''Shibayama Kofun Group''' and the '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' can be found. The river then '''exits the valley at Shibahara''', flowing into the '''Kujūkuri Plain''', where it meanders through '''rice fields'''.
Both banks of the river feature '''high ridges''', supporting '''thriving aquatic plant life'''. After passing '''Ryokai Bridge''', the river '''empties into the Pacific Ocean''', bordering '''Kujūkuri Beach'''. The '''Shirahata Beach (west side)''' and '''Minamihama Beach (east side)''' are separated by the river’s mouth.
The river is '''21.5 km long''', with '''20.9 km (from Makino, Shibayama Town, to the mouth) designated as a secondary river'''.
=== Naming === Originally, the Kido River had '''no fixed name'''. Different localities referred to the river by '''various names''': * '''Takoe River''' (*Takoegawa*) in '''Takoe Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Karumo Arai River''' (*Karumo Shingawa*) in '''Karumo Hongō Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Kido River''' (*Kido-gawa*) in '''Kido Village''' (now '''Sanmu City''')
Among these, '''"Kido River"''' became the widely accepted name.
== Flood Control == In '''1966 (Shōwa 41)''', '''a large-scale river improvement project''' was initiated to '''expand and fortify''' the river. The initial phase '''(1966–1972)''' focused on '''4 km from the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge to Komatsu Bridge'''. Later, improvements were made to the '''middle and lower sections''', with the project being completed in '''1997 (Heisei 9)'''.
After completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:, further work '''remained paused''' except for a '''levee heightening project''' in '''2009''' near the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge.
=== 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake & Tsunami Impact === During the '''2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami''', the Kido River '''sustained significant damage''', surpassing '''all other nearby rivers''' in terms of destruction. The '''tsunami from Iinoura Coast breached the sea wall''' and traveled '''8 km upstream''', reaching '''Takoebashi Bridge'''.<ref>[http://wwwp.pref.chiba.lg.jp/pbgikai/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest06!!25275!1!1!318,-1,318!1986!159440!318,-1,318!1986!159440!324,323,322!14!23!144606!65!1?Template=DocOneFrame Chiba Prefecture Assembly Records – Heisei 24 (2012), 9th Session]</ref>
The '''tsunami destroyed levees in five locations near the river mouth''' and caused '''bank collapses in the middle reaches'''. This '''exposed weaknesses in the flood control system''', leading to a '''1-meter levee reinforcement project''' starting in '''July 2011'''. The '''first phase''' (non-land acquisition areas) was completed in '''December 2012''', while the remaining sections were scheduled for completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: by '''March 2014'''.<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 23 (2011), 3rd Session, Sept 6]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 24 (2012), 3rd Session, Sept 11]</ref>
A '''proposal for using nearby vacant land as evacuation routes''' was also introduced. Meanwhile, the '''upper course levee reinforcement project''' has continued beyond 2009.
=== Environmental Preservation === The '''"Protect the Beautiful Kido River" Committee''' was founded in '''May 1992''', engaging in '''awareness campaigns and river clean-up efforts'''.<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf "Environmental Report of Narita City – Heisei 24 (2012)"]</ref>
== Major Bridges == * '''Kawazuba Bridge''' – [[Japan National Route 296|Route 296]] * '''Koike Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 45|Route 45]] * '''Meiji Bridge''' * '''Matsuba Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 116|Route 116]] * '''Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway (Ken-Ō Expressway)''' * '''Kowa Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 112|Route 112]] * '''Kowa 1st Bridge''' * '''Kowa 5th Bridge''' * '''Kowa 4th Bridge''' * '''Kanao 1st Bridge''' * '''Kanao 2nd Bridge''' * '''Benten Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 22|Route 22]] * '''Kase Bridge''' * '''Ōkura Bridge''' * '''Takoebashi Bridge''' * '''Shin-Takoebashi Bridge''' – [[Japan National Route 126|Route 126]] * '''JR Sōbu Main Line Bridge''' * '''Rokumanbu Bridge''' * '''Nakadai Bridge''' * '''Sakae Bridge''' * '''Komatsu Bridge''' * '''Orito Bridge''' * '''Kido Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 122|Route 122]] * '''Ryokai Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 30|Route 30]]

=== Environmental Preservation === The '''"Protect the Beautiful Kido River" Committee''' was founded in '''May 1992''', engaging in '''awareness campaigns and river clean-up efforts'''.<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf "Environmental Report of Narita City – Heisei 24 (2012)"]</ref>

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Kido River in the Kujūkuri region|the tributary of the Tonegawa system in Funabashi City|Kido River (Funabashi)}} {{Infobox river | name = Kido River | image = [[File:Kidoriver2.jpg|300px]] | image_caption = View downstream from Kido Bridge | watercourse = [[Secondary watercourse (Japan)|Secondary watercourse]] | watercourse_system = Kido River system | type = [[Secondary river]] | length = 21.5 km | elevation = 35 m | discharge = | observation_station = | basin_area = 72.1 km² | source = [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]], Honjō area | mouth = [[Pacific Ocean]] (Sanmu City) | basin = [[Sanmu, Chiba|Sanmu City]], [[Sanbu District, Chiba|Sanbu District]] (Shibayama Town, Yokoshibahikari Town), [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]] | footnotes = | source_url = [https://www.maff.go.jp/kanto/nouson/sekkei/kokuei/ryoso/shizen/01_2.html] }} [[File:Kidoriver1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Upstream view from Kido Bridge]] '''Kido River''' (木戸川, *Kido-gawa*) is a [[secondary river]] flowing through the '''central Kujūkuri Plain''' in '''northeastern Chiba Prefecture, Japan'''. It is the main river of the '''Kido River system'''.
== Geography == The Kido River originates in '''Narita City’s Honjō area''', where it is fed by an '''adjustment pond''', before flowing '''southeast'''. It runs along the '''border of Shibayama Town''' in Sanbu District and then '''passes through the western part of the town''', where it is joined by '''several small tributaries''' from the west.
Exiting Shibayama, the river marks the '''boundary between Yokoshibahikari Town and Sanmu City''' before entering '''Sanmu City'''. Along its course, '''historical sites''' such as the '''Shibayama Kofun Group''' and the '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' can be found. The river then '''exits the valley at Shibahara''', flowing into the '''Kujūkuri Plain''', where it meanders through '''rice fields'''.
Both banks of the river feature '''high ridges''', supporting '''thriving aquatic plant life'''. After passing '''Ryokai Bridge''', the river '''empties into the Pacific Ocean''', bordering '''Kujūkuri Beach'''. The '''Shirahata Beach (west side)''' and '''Minamihama Beach (east side)''' are separated by the river’s mouth.
The river is '''21.5 km long''', with '''20.9 km (from Makino, Shibayama Town, to the mouth) designated as a secondary river'''.
=== Naming === Originally, the Kido River had '''no fixed name'''. Different localities referred to the river by '''various names''': * '''Takoe River''' (*Takoegawa*) in '''Takoe Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Karumo Arai River''' (*Karumo Shingawa*) in '''Karumo Hongō Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Kido River''' (*Kido-gawa*) in '''Kido Village''' (now '''Sanmu City''')
Among these, '''"Kido River"''' became the widely accepted name.
== Flood Control == In '''1966 (Shōwa 41)''', '''a large-scale river improvement project''' was initiated to '''expand and fortify''' the river. The initial phase '''(1966–1972)''' focused on '''4 km from the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge to Komatsu Bridge'''. Later, improvements were made to the '''middle and lower sections''', with the project being completed in '''1997 (Heisei 9)'''.
After completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:, further work '''remained paused''' except for a '''levee heightening project''' in '''2009''' near the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge.
=== 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake & Tsunami Impact === During the '''2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami''', the Kido River '''sustained significant damage''', surpassing '''all other nearby rivers''' in terms of destruction. The '''tsunami from Iinoura Coast breached the sea wall''' and traveled '''8 km upstream''', reaching '''Takoebashi Bridge'''.<ref>[http://wwwp.pref.chiba.lg.jp/pbgikai/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest06!!25275!1!1!318,-1,318!1986!159440!318,-1,318!1986!159440!324,323,322!14!23!144606!65!1?Template=DocOneFrame Chiba Prefecture Assembly Records – Heisei 24 (2012), 9th Session]</ref>
The '''tsunami destroyed levees in five locations near the river mouth''' and caused '''bank collapses in the middle reaches'''. This '''exposed weaknesses in the flood control system''', leading to a '''1-meter levee reinforcement project''' starting in '''July 2011'''. The '''first phase''' (non-land acquisition areas) was completed in '''December 2012''', while the remaining sections were scheduled for completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: by '''March 2014'''.<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 23 (2011), 3rd Session, Sept 6]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 24 (2012), 3rd Session, Sept 11]</ref>
A '''proposal for using nearby vacant land as evacuation routes''' was also introduced. Meanwhile, the '''upper course levee reinforcement project''' has continued beyond 2009.
=== Environmental Preservation === The '''"Protect the Beautiful Kido River" Committee''' was founded in '''May 1992''', engaging in '''awareness campaigns and river clean-up efforts'''.<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf "Environmental Report of Narita City – Heisei 24 (2012)"]</ref>
== Major Bridges == * '''Kawazuba Bridge''' – [[Japan National Route 296|Route 296]] * '''Koike Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 45|Route 45]] * '''Meiji Bridge''' * '''Matsuba Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 116|Route 116]] * '''Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway (Ken-Ō Expressway)''' * '''Kowa Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 112|Route 112]] * '''Kowa 1st Bridge''' * '''Kowa 5th Bridge''' * '''Kowa 4th Bridge''' * '''Kanao 1st Bridge''' * '''Kanao 2nd Bridge''' * '''Benten Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 22|Route 22]] * '''Kase Bridge''' * '''Ōkura Bridge''' * '''Takoebashi Bridge''' * '''Shin-Takoebashi Bridge''' – [[Japan National Route 126|Route 126]] * '''JR Sōbu Main Line Bridge''' * '''Rokumanbu Bridge''' * '''Nakadai Bridge''' * '''Sakae Bridge''' * '''Komatsu Bridge''' * '''Orito Bridge''' * '''Kido Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 122|Route 122]] * '''Ryokai Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 30|Route 30]]
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also! == * [[List of secondary rivers in Chiba Prefecture]]

[[Category:11]][[sn:Kido River (Chiba)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kido River (Chiba)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kido River (Chiba)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kido River (Chiba)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Kido River (Chiba)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kido River (Chiba)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kido River (Chiba)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kido River (Chiba)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kido River (Chiba)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kido River (Chiba)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ido River (Chiba)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kido River (Chiba)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kido River (Chiba)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kido River (Chiba)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

== Major Bridges == * '''Kawazuba Bridge''' – [[Japan National Route 296|Route 296]] * '''Koike Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 45|Route 45]] * '''Meiji Bridge''' * '''Matsuba Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 116|Route 116]] * '''Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway (Ken-Ō Expressway)''' * '''Kowa Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 112|Route 112]] * '''Kowa 1st Bridge''' * '''Kowa 5th Bridge''' * '''Kowa 4th Bridge''' * '''Kanao 1st Bridge''' * '''Kanao 2nd Bridge''' * '''Benten Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 22|Route 22]] * '''Kase Bridge''' * '''Ōkura Bridge''' * '''Takoebashi Bridge''' * '''Shin-Takoebashi Bridge''' – [[Japan National Route 126|Route 126]] * '''JR Sōbu Main Line Bridge''' * '''Rokumanbu Bridge''' * '''Nakadai Bridge''' * '''Sakae Bridge''' * '''Komatsu Bridge''' * '''Orito Bridge''' * '''Kido Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 122|Route 122]] * '''Ryokai Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 30|Route 30]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category: " to "[[Category:"
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Kido River in the Kujūkuri region|the tributary of the Tonegawa system in Funabashi City|Kido River (Funabashi)}} {{Infobox river | name = Kido River | image = [[File:Kidoriver2.jpg|300px]] | image_caption = View downstream from Kido Bridge | watercourse = [[Secondary watercourse (Japan)|Secondary watercourse]] | watercourse_system = Kido River system | type = [[Secondary river]] | length = 21.5 km | elevation = 35 m | discharge = | observation_station = | basin_area = 72.1 km² | source = [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]], Honjō area | mouth = [[Pacific Ocean]] (Sanmu City) | basin = [[Sanmu, Chiba|Sanmu City]], [[Sanbu District, Chiba|Sanbu District]] (Shibayama Town, Yokoshibahikari Town), [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]] | footnotes = | source_url = [https://www.maff.go.jp/kanto/nouson/sekkei/kokuei/ryoso/shizen/01_2.html] }} [[File:Kidoriver1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Upstream view from Kido Bridge]] '''Kido River''' (木戸川, *Kido-gawa*) is a [[secondary river]] flowing through the '''central Kujūkuri Plain''' in '''northeastern Chiba Prefecture, Japan'''. It is the main river of the '''Kido River system'''.
== Geography == The Kido River originates in '''Narita City’s Honjō area''', where it is fed by an '''adjustment pond''', before flowing '''southeast'''. It runs along the '''border of Shibayama Town''' in Sanbu District and then '''passes through the western part of the town''', where it is joined by '''several small tributaries''' from the west.
Exiting Shibayama, the river marks the '''boundary between Yokoshibahikari Town and Sanmu City''' before entering '''Sanmu City'''. Along its course, '''historical sites''' such as the '''Shibayama Kofun Group''' and the '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' can be found. The river then '''exits the valley at Shibahara''', flowing into the '''Kujūkuri Plain''', where it meanders through '''rice fields'''.
Both banks of the river feature '''high ridges''', supporting '''thriving aquatic plant life'''. After passing '''Ryokai Bridge''', the river '''empties into the Pacific Ocean''', bordering '''Kujūkuri Beach'''. The '''Shirahata Beach (west side)''' and '''Minamihama Beach (east side)''' are separated by the river’s mouth.
The river is '''21.5 km long''', with '''20.9 km (from Makino, Shibayama Town, to the mouth) designated as a secondary river'''.
=== Naming === Originally, the Kido River had '''no fixed name'''. Different localities referred to the river by '''various names''': * '''Takoe River''' (*Takoegawa*) in '''Takoe Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Karumo Arai River''' (*Karumo Shingawa*) in '''Karumo Hongō Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Kido River''' (*Kido-gawa*) in '''Kido Village''' (now '''Sanmu City''')
Among these, '''"Kido River"''' became the widely accepted name.
== Flood Control == In '''1966 (Shōwa 41)''', '''a large-scale river improvement project''' was initiated to '''expand and fortify''' the river. The initial phase '''(1966–1972)''' focused on '''4 km from the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge to Komatsu Bridge'''. Later, improvements were made to the '''middle and lower sections''', with the project being completed in '''1997 (Heisei 9)'''.
After completion tier 6]][[Category:tier 6]][[Category:, further work '''remained paused''' except for a '''levee heightening project''' in '''2009''' near the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge.
=== 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake & Tsunami Impact === During the '''2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami''', the Kido River '''sustained significant damage''', surpassing '''all other nearby rivers''' in terms of destruction. The '''tsunami from Iinoura Coast breached the sea wall''' and traveled '''8 km upstream''', reaching '''Takoebashi Bridge'''.<ref>[http://wwwp.pref.chiba.lg.jp/pbgikai/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest06!!25275!1!1!318,-1,318!1986!159440!318,-1,318!1986!159440!324,323,322!14!23!144606!65!1?Template=DocOneFrame Chiba Prefecture Assembly Records – Heisei 24 (2012), 9th Session]</ref>
The '''tsunami destroyed levees in five locations near the river mouth''' and caused '''bank collapses in the middle reaches'''. This '''exposed weaknesses in the flood control system''', leading to a '''1-meter levee reinforcement project''' starting in '''July 2011'''. The '''first phase''' (non-land acquisition areas) was completed in '''December 2012''', while the remaining sections were scheduled for completion tier 6]][[Category:tier 6]][[Category:by '''March 2014'''.<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 23 (2011), 3rd Session, Sept 6]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 24 (2012), 3rd Session, Sept 11]</ref>
A '''proposal for using nearby vacant land as evacuation routes''' was also introduced. Meanwhile, the '''upper course levee reinforcement project''' has continued beyond 2009.
=== Environmental Preservation === The '''"Protect the Beautiful Kido River" Committee''' was founded in '''May 1992''', engaging in '''awareness campaigns and river clean-up efforts'''.<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf "Environmental Report of Narita City – Heisei 24 (2012)"]</ref>
== Major Bridges == * '''Kawazuba Bridge''' – [[Japan National Route 296|Route 296]] * '''Koike Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 45|Route 45]] * '''Meiji Bridge''' * '''Matsuba Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 116|Route 116]] * '''Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway (Ken-Ō Expressway)''' * '''Kowa Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 112|Route 112]] * '''Kowa 1st Bridge''' * '''Kowa 5th Bridge''' * '''Kowa 4th Bridge''' * '''Kanao 1st Bridge''' * '''Kanao 2nd Bridge''' * '''Benten Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 22|Route 22]] * '''Kase Bridge''' * '''Ōkura Bridge''' * '''Takoebashi Bridge''' * '''Shin-Takoebashi Bridge''' – [[Japan National Route 126|Route 126]] * '''JR Sōbu Main Line Bridge''' * '''Rokumanbu Bridge''' * '''Nakadai Bridge''' * '''Sakae Bridge''' * '''Komatsu Bridge''' * '''Orito Bridge''' * '''Kido Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 122|Route 122]] * '''Ryokai Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 30|Route 30]]
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of secondary rivers in Chiba Prefecture]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Kido River in the Kujūkuri region|the tributary of the Tonegawa system in Funabashi City|Kido River (Funabashi)}} {{Infobox river | name = Kido River | image = [[File:Kidoriver2.jpg|300px]] | image_caption = View downstream from Kido Bridge | watercourse = [[Secondary watercourse (Japan)|Secondary watercourse]] | watercourse_system = Kido River system | type = [[Secondary river]] | length = 21.5 km | elevation = 35 m | discharge = | observation_station = | basin_area = 72.1 km² | source = [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]], Honjō area | mouth = [[Pacific Ocean]] (Sanmu City) | basin = [[Sanmu, Chiba|Sanmu City]], [[Sanbu District, Chiba|Sanbu District]] (Shibayama Town, Yokoshibahikari Town), [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]] | footnotes = | source_url = [https://www.maff.go.jp/kanto/nouson/sekkei/kokuei/ryoso/shizen/01_2.html] }} [[File:Kidoriver1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Upstream view from Kido Bridge]] '''Kido River''' (木戸川, *Kido-gawa*) is a [[secondary river]] flowing through the '''central Kujūkuri Plain''' in '''northeastern Chiba Prefecture, Japan'''. It is the main river of the '''Kido River system'''.
== Geography == The Kido River originates in '''Narita City’s Honjō area''', where it is fed by an '''adjustment pond''', before flowing '''southeast'''. It runs along the '''border of Shibayama Town''' in Sanbu District and then '''passes through the western part of the town''', where it is joined by '''several small tributaries''' from the west.
Exiting Shibayama, the river marks the '''boundary between Yokoshibahikari Town and Sanmu City''' before entering '''Sanmu City'''. Along its course, '''historical sites''' such as the '''Shibayama Kofun Group''' and the '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' can be found. The river then '''exits the valley at Shibahara''', flowing into the '''Kujūkuri Plain''', where it meanders through '''rice fields'''.
Both banks of the river feature '''high ridges''', supporting '''thriving aquatic plant life'''. After passing '''Ryokai Bridge''', the river '''empties into the Pacific Ocean''', bordering '''Kujūkuri Beach'''. The '''Shirahata Beach (west side)''' and '''Minamihama Beach (east side)''' are separated by the river’s mouth.
The river is '''21.5 km long''', with '''20.9 km (from Makino, Shibayama Town, to the mouth) designated as a secondary river'''.
=== Naming === Originally, the Kido River had '''no fixed name'''. Different localities referred to the river by '''various names''': * '''Takoe River''' (*Takoegawa*) in '''Takoe Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Karumo Arai River''' (*Karumo Shingawa*) in '''Karumo Hongō Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Kido River''' (*Kido-gawa*) in '''Kido Village''' (now '''Sanmu City''')
Among these, '''"Kido River"''' became the widely accepted name.
== Flood Control == In '''1966 (Shōwa 41)''', '''a large-scale river improvement project''' was initiated to '''expand and fortify''' the river. The initial phase '''(1966–1972)''' focused on '''4 km from the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge to Komatsu Bridge'''. Later, improvements were made to the '''middle and lower sections''', with the project being completed in '''1997 (Heisei 9)'''.
After completion tier 6]][[Category:, further work '''remained paused''' except for a '''levee heightening project''' in '''2009''' near the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge.
=== 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake & Tsunami Impact === During the '''2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami''', the Kido River '''sustained significant damage''', surpassing '''all other nearby rivers''' in terms of destruction. The '''tsunami from Iinoura Coast breached the sea wall''' and traveled '''8 km upstream''', reaching '''Takoebashi Bridge'''.<ref>[http://wwwp.pref.chiba.lg.jp/pbgikai/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest06!!25275!1!1!318,-1,318!1986!159440!318,-1,318!1986!159440!324,323,322!14!23!144606!65!1?Template=DocOneFrame Chiba Prefecture Assembly Records – Heisei 24 (2012), 9th Session]</ref>
The '''tsunami destroyed levees in five locations near the river mouth''' and caused '''bank collapses in the middle reaches'''. This '''exposed weaknesses in the flood control system''', leading to a '''1-meter levee reinforcement project''' starting in '''July 2011'''. The '''first phase''' (non-land acquisition areas) was completed in '''December 2012''', while the remaining sections were scheduled for completion tier 6]][[Category:by '''March 2014'''.<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 23 (2011), 3rd Session, Sept 6]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 24 (2012), 3rd Session, Sept 11]</ref>
A '''proposal for using nearby vacant land as evacuation routes''' was also introduced. Meanwhile, the '''upper course levee reinforcement project''' has continued beyond 2009.
=== Environmental Preservation === The '''"Protect the Beautiful Kido River" Committee''' was founded in '''May 1992''', engaging in '''awareness campaigns and river clean-up efforts'''.<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf "Environmental Report of Narita City – Heisei 24 (2012)"]</ref>
== Major Bridges == * '''Kawazuba Bridge''' – [[Japan National Route 296|Route 296]] * '''Koike Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 45|Route 45]] * '''Meiji Bridge''' * '''Matsuba Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 116|Route 116]] * '''Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway (Ken-Ō Expressway)''' * '''Kowa Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 112|Route 112]] * '''Kowa 1st Bridge''' * '''Kowa 5th Bridge''' * '''Kowa 4th Bridge''' * '''Kanao 1st Bridge''' * '''Kanao 2nd Bridge''' * '''Benten Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 22|Route 22]] * '''Kase Bridge''' * '''Ōkura Bridge''' * '''Takoebashi Bridge''' * '''Shin-Takoebashi Bridge''' – [[Japan National Route 126|Route 126]] * '''JR Sōbu Main Line Bridge''' * '''Rokumanbu Bridge''' * '''Nakadai Bridge''' * '''Sakae Bridge''' * '''Komatsu Bridge''' * '''Orito Bridge''' * '''Kido Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 122|Route 122]] * '''Ryokai Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 30|Route 30]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of secondary rivers in Chiba Prefecture]]
{{citation needed|Kido River}}

The '''first phase''' (non-land acquisition areas) was completed in '''December 2012''', while the remaining sections were scheduled for completion tier 6]][[Category:by '''March 2014'''.<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 23 (2011), 3rd Session, Sept 6]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 24 (2012), 3rd Session, Sept 11]</ref>
A '''proposal for using nearby vacant land as evacuation routes''' was also introduced. Meanwhile, the '''upper course levee reinforcement project''' has continued beyond 2009.
=== Environmental Preservation === The '''"Protect the Beautiful Kido River" Committee''' was founded in '''May 1992''', engaging in '''awareness campaigns and river clean-up efforts'''.<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf "Environmental Report of Narita City – Heisei 24 (2012)"]</ref>
== Major Bridges == * '''Kawazuba Bridge''' – [[Japan National Route 296|Route 296]] * '''Koike Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 45|Route 45]] * '''Meiji Bridge''' * '''Matsuba Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 116|Route 116]] * '''Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway (Ken-Ō Expressway)''' * '''Kowa Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 112|Route 112]] * '''Kowa 1st Bridge''' * '''Kowa 5th Bridge''' * '''Kowa 4th Bridge''' * '''Kanao 1st Bridge''' * '''Kanao 2nd Bridge''' * '''Benten Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 22|Route 22]] * '''Kase Bridge''' * '''Ōkura Bridge''' * '''Takoebashi Bridge''' * '''Shin-Takoebashi Bridge''' – [[Japan National Route 126|Route 126]] * '''JR Sōbu Main Line Bridge''' * '''Rokumanbu Bridge''' * '''Nakadai Bridge''' * '''Sakae Bridge''' * '''Komatsu Bridge''' * '''Orito Bridge''' * '''Kido Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 122|Route 122]] * '''Ryokai Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 30|Route 30]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Kido River in the Kujūkuri region|the tributary of the Tonegawa system in Funabashi City|Kido River (Funabashi)}} {{Infobox river | name = Kido River | image = [[File:Kidoriver2.jpg|300px]] | image_caption = View downstream from Kido Bridge | watercourse = [[Secondary watercourse (Japan)|Secondary watercourse]] | watercourse_system = Kido River system | type = [[Secondary river]] | length = 21.5 km | elevation = 35 m | discharge = | observation_station = | basin_area = 72.1 km² | source = [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]], Honjō area | mouth = [[Pacific Ocean]] (Sanmu City) | basin = [[Sanmu, Chiba|Sanmu City]], [[Sanbu District, Chiba|Sanbu District]] (Shibayama Town, Yokoshibahikari Town), [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]] | footnotes = | source_url = [https://www.maff.go.jp/kanto/nouson/sekkei/kokuei/ryoso/shizen/01_2.html] }} [[File:Kidoriver1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Upstream view from Kido Bridge]] '''Kido River''' (木戸川, *Kido-gawa*) is a [[secondary river]] flowing through the '''central Kujūkuri Plain''' in '''northeastern Chiba Prefecture, Japan'''. It is the main river of the '''Kido River system'''.
== Geography == The Kido River originates in '''Narita City’s Honjō area''', where it is fed by an '''adjustment pond''', before flowing '''southeast'''. It runs along the '''border of Shibayama Town''' in Sanbu District and then '''passes through the western part of the town''', where it is joined by '''several small tributaries''' from the west.
Exiting Shibayama, the river marks the '''boundary between Yokoshibahikari Town and Sanmu City''' before entering '''Sanmu City'''. Along its course, '''historical sites''' such as the '''Shibayama Kofun Group''' and the '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' can be found. The river then '''exits the valley at Shibahara''', flowing into the '''Kujūkuri Plain''', where it meanders through '''rice fields'''.
Both banks of the river feature '''high ridges''', supporting '''thriving aquatic plant life'''. After passing '''Ryokai Bridge''', the river '''empties into the Pacific Ocean''', bordering '''Kujūkuri Beach'''. The '''Shirahata Beach (west side)''' and '''Minamihama Beach (east side)''' are separated by the river’s mouth.
The river is '''21.5 km long''', with '''20.9 km (from Makino, Shibayama Town, to the mouth) designated as a secondary river'''.
=== Naming === Originally, the Kido River had '''no fixed name'''. Different localities referred to the river by '''various names''': * '''Takoe River''' (*Takoegawa*) in '''Takoe Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Karumo Arai River''' (*Karumo Shingawa*) in '''Karumo Hongō Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Kido River''' (*Kido-gawa*) in '''Kido Village''' (now '''Sanmu City''')
Among these, '''"Kido River"''' became the widely accepted name.
== Flood Control == In '''1966 (Shōwa 41)''', '''a large-scale river improvement project''' was initiated to '''expand and fortify''' the river. The initial phase '''(1966–1972)''' focused on '''4 km from the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge to Komatsu Bridge'''. Later, improvements were made to the '''middle and lower sections''', with the project being completed in '''1997 (Heisei 9)'''.
After completion tier 6]][[Category:, further work '''remained paused''' except for a '''levee heightening project''' in '''2009''' near the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge.
=== 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake & Tsunami Impact === During the '''2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami''', the Kido River '''sustained significant damage''', surpassing '''all other nearby rivers''' in terms of destruction. The '''tsunami from Iinoura Coast breached the sea wall''' and traveled '''8 km upstream''', reaching '''Takoebashi Bridge'''.<ref>[http://wwwp.pref.chiba.lg.jp/pbgikai/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest06!!25275!1!1!318,-1,318!1986!159440!318,-1,318!1986!159440!324,323,322!14!23!144606!65!1?Template=DocOneFrame Chiba Prefecture Assembly Records – Heisei 24 (2012), 9th Session]</ref>
The '''tsunami destroyed levees in five locations near the river mouth''' and caused '''bank collapses in the middle reaches'''. This '''exposed weaknesses in the flood control system''', leading to a '''1-meter levee reinforcement project''' starting in '''July 2011'''. The '''first phase''' (non-land acquisition areas) was completed in '''December 2012''', while the remaining sections were scheduled for completion tier 6]][[Category:by '''March 2014'''.<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 23 (2011), 3rd Session, Sept 6]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 24 (2012), 3rd Session, Sept 11]</ref>
A '''proposal for using nearby vacant land as evacuation routes''' was also introduced. Meanwhile, the '''upper course levee reinforcement project''' has continued beyond 2009.
=== Environmental Preservation === The '''"Protect the Beautiful Kido River" Committee''' was founded in '''May 1992''', engaging in '''awareness campaigns and river clean-up efforts'''.<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf "Environmental Report of Narita City – Heisei 24 (2012)"]</ref>
== Major Bridges == * '''Kawazuba Bridge''' – [[Japan National Route 296|Route 296]] * '''Koike Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 45|Route 45]] * '''Meiji Bridge''' * '''Matsuba Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 116|Route 116]] * '''Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway (Ken-Ō Expressway)''' * '''Kowa Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 112|Route 112]] * '''Kowa 1st Bridge''' * '''Kowa 5th Bridge''' * '''Kowa 4th Bridge''' * '''Kanao 1st Bridge''' * '''Kanao 2nd Bridge''' * '''Benten Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 22|Route 22]] * '''Kase Bridge''' * '''Ōkura Bridge''' * '''Takoebashi Bridge''' * '''Shin-Takoebashi Bridge''' – [[Japan National Route 126|Route 126]] * '''JR Sōbu Main Line Bridge''' * '''Rokumanbu Bridge''' * '''Nakadai Bridge''' * '''Sakae Bridge''' * '''Komatsu Bridge''' * '''Orito Bridge''' * '''Kido Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 122|Route 122]] * '''Ryokai Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 30|Route 30]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of secondary rivers in Chiba Prefecture]]
{{citation needed|Kido River}}

Both banks of the river feature '''high ridges''', supporting '''thriving aquatic plant life'''. After passing '''Ryokai Bridge''', the river '''empties into the Pacific Ocean''', bordering '''Kujūkuri Beach'''. The '''Shirahata Beach (west side)''' and '''Minamihama Beach (east side)''' are separated by the river’s mouth.
The river is '''21.5 km long''', with '''20.9 km (from Makino, Shibayama Town, to the mouth) designated as a secondary river'''.
=== Naming === Originally, the Kido River had '''no fixed name'''. Different localities referred to the river by '''various names''': * '''Takoe River''' (*Takoegawa*) in '''Takoe Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Karumo Arai River''' (*Karumo Shingawa*) in '''Karumo Hongō Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Kido River''' (*Kido-gawa*) in '''Kido Village''' (now '''Sanmu City''')
Among these, '''"Kido River"''' became the widely accepted name.
== Flood Control == In '''1966 (Shōwa 41)''', '''a large-scale river improvement project''' was initiated to '''expand and fortify''' the river. The initial phase '''(1966–1972)''' focused on '''4 km from the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge to Komatsu Bridge'''. Later, improvements were made to the '''middle and lower sections''', with the project being completed in '''1997 (Heisei 9)'''.
After Completion tier 6]][[Category:, further work '''remained paused''' except for a '''levee heightening project''' in '''2009''' near the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge.
=== 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake & Tsunami Impact === During the '''2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami''', the Kido River '''sustained significant damage''', surpassing '''all other nearby rivers''' in terms of destruction. The '''tsunami from Iinoura Coast breached the sea wall''' and traveled '''8 km upstream''', reaching '''Takoebashi Bridge'''.<ref>[http://wwwp.pref.chiba.lg.jp/pbgikai/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest06!!25275!1!1!318,-1,318!1986!159440!318,-1,318!1986!159440!324,323,322!14!23!144606!65!1?Template=DocOneFrame Chiba Prefecture Assembly Records – Heisei 24 (2012), 9th Session]</ref>
The '''tsunami destroyed levees in five locations near the river mouth''' and caused '''bank collapses in the middle reaches'''. This '''exposed weaknesses in the flood control system''', leading to a '''1-meter levee reinforcement project''' starting in '''July 2011'''. The '''first phase''' (non-land acquisition areas) was completed in '''December 2012''', while the remaining sections were scheduled for Completion tier 6]][[Category:by '''March 2014'''.<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 23 (2011), 3rd Session, Sept 6]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 24 (2012), 3rd Session, Sept 11]</ref>
A '''proposal for using nearby vacant land as evacuation routes''' was also introduced. Meanwhile, the '''upper course levee reinforcement project''' has continued beyond 2009.
=== Environmental Preservation === The '''"Protect the Beautiful Kido River" Committee''' was founded in '''May 1992''', engaging in '''awareness campaigns and river clean-up efforts'''.<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf "Environmental Report of Narita City – Heisei 24 (2012)"]</ref>
== Major Bridges == * '''Kawazuba Bridge''' – [[Japan National Route 296|Route 296]] * '''Koike Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 45|Route 45]] * '''Meiji Bridge''' * '''Matsuba Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 116|Route 116]] * '''Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway (Ken-Ō Expressway)''' * '''Kowa Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 112|Route 112]] * '''Kowa 1st Bridge''' * '''Kowa 5th Bridge''' * '''Kowa 4th Bridge''' * '''Kanao 1st Bridge''' * '''Kanao 2nd Bridge''' * '''Benten Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 22|Route 22]] * '''Kase Bridge''' * '''Ōkura Bridge''' * '''Takoebashi Bridge''' * '''Shin-Takoebashi Bridge''' – [[Japan National Route 126|Route 126]] * '''JR Sōbu Main Line Bridge''' * '''Rokumanbu Bridge''' * '''Nakadai Bridge''' * '''Sakae Bridge''' * '''Komatsu Bridge''' * '''Orito Bridge''' * '''Kido Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 122|Route 122]] * '''Ryokai Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 30|Route 30]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of secondary rivers in Chiba Prefecture]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Kido River in the Kujūkuri region|the tributary of the Tonegawa system in Funabashi City|Kido River (Funabashi)}} {{Infobox river | name = Kido River | image = [[File:Kidoriver2.jpg|300px]] | image_caption = View downstream from Kido Bridge | watercourse = [[Secondary watercourse (Japan)|Secondary watercourse]] | watercourse_system = Kido River system | type = [[Secondary river]] | length = 21.5 km | elevation = 35 m | discharge = | observation_station = | basin_area = 72.1 km² | source = [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]], Honjō area | mouth = [[Pacific Ocean]] (Sanmu City) | basin = [[Sanmu, Chiba|Sanmu City]], [[Sanbu District, Chiba|Sanbu District]] (Shibayama Town, Yokoshibahikari Town), [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]] | footnotes = | source_url = [https://www.maff.go.jp/kanto/nouson/sekkei/kokuei/ryoso/shizen/01_2.html] }} [[File:Kidoriver1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Upstream view from Kido Bridge]] '''Kido River''' (木戸川, *Kido-gawa*) is a [[secondary river]] flowing through the '''central Kujūkuri Plain''' in '''northeastern Chiba Prefecture, Japan'''. It is the main river of the '''Kido River system'''.
== Geography == The Kido River originates in '''Narita City’s Honjō area''', where it is fed by an '''adjustment pond''', before flowing '''southeast'''. It runs along the '''border of Shibayama Town''' in Sanbu District and then '''passes through the western part of the town''', where it is joined by '''several small tributaries''' from the west.
Exiting Shibayama, the river marks the '''boundary between Yokoshibahikari Town and Sanmu City''' before entering '''Sanmu City'''. Along its course, '''historical sites''' such as the '''Shibayama Kofun Group''' and the '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' can be found. The river then '''exits the valley at Shibahara''', flowing into the '''Kujūkuri Plain''', where it meanders through '''rice fields'''.
Both banks of the river feature '''high ridges''', supporting '''thriving aquatic plant life'''. After passing '''Ryokai Bridge''', the river '''empties into the Pacific Ocean''', bordering '''Kujūkuri Beach'''. The '''Shirahata Beach (west side)''' and '''Minamihama Beach (east side)''' are separated by the river’s mouth.
The river is '''21.5 km long''', with '''20.9 km (from Makino, Shibayama Town, to the mouth) designated as a secondary river'''.
=== Naming === Originally, the Kido River had '''no fixed name'''. Different localities referred to the river by '''various names''': * '''Takoe River''' (*Takoegawa*) in '''Takoe Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Karumo Arai River''' (*Karumo Shingawa*) in '''Karumo Hongō Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Kido River''' (*Kido-gawa*) in '''Kido Village''' (now '''Sanmu City''')
Among these, '''"Kido River"''' became the widely accepted name.
== Flood Control == In '''1966 (Shōwa 41)''', '''a large-scale river improvement project''' was initiated to '''expand and fortify''' the river. The initial phase '''(1966–1972)''' focused on '''4 km from the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge to Komatsu Bridge'''. Later, improvements were made to the '''middle and lower sections''', with the project being completed in '''1997 (Heisei 9)'''.
After Completion tier 6]][[Category:, further work '''remained paused''' except for a '''levee heightening project''' in '''2009''' near the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge.
=== 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake & Tsunami Impact === During the '''2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami''', the Kido River '''sustained significant damage''', surpassing '''all other nearby rivers''' in terms of destruction. The '''tsunami from Iinoura Coast breached the sea wall''' and traveled '''8 km upstream''', reaching '''Takoebashi Bridge'''.<ref>[http://wwwp.pref.chiba.lg.jp/pbgikai/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest06!!25275!1!1!318,-1,318!1986!159440!318,-1,318!1986!159440!324,323,322!14!23!144606!65!1?Template=DocOneFrame Chiba Prefecture Assembly Records – Heisei 24 (2012), 9th Session]</ref>
The '''tsunami destroyed levees in five locations near the river mouth''' and caused '''bank collapses in the middle reaches'''. This '''exposed weaknesses in the flood control system''', leading to a '''1-meter levee reinforcement project''' starting in '''July 2011'''. The '''first phase''' (non-land acquisition areas) was completed in '''December 2012''', while the remaining sections were scheduled for Completion tier 6]][[Category:by '''March 2014'''.<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 23 (2011), 3rd Session, Sept 6]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 24 (2012), 3rd Session, Sept 11]</ref>
A '''proposal for using nearby vacant land as evacuation routes''' was also introduced. Meanwhile, the '''upper course levee reinforcement project''' has continued beyond 2009.
=== Environmental Preservation === The '''"Protect the Beautiful Kido River" Committee''' was founded in '''May 1992''', engaging in '''awareness campaigns and river clean-up efforts'''.<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf "Environmental Report of Narita City – Heisei 24 (2012)"]</ref>
== Major Bridges == * '''Kawazuba Bridge''' – [[Japan National Route 296|Route 296]] * '''Koike Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 45|Route 45]] * '''Meiji Bridge''' * '''Matsuba Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 116|Route 116]] * '''Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway (Ken-Ō Expressway)''' * '''Kowa Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 112|Route 112]] * '''Kowa 1st Bridge''' * '''Kowa 5th Bridge''' * '''Kowa 4th Bridge''' * '''Kanao 1st Bridge''' * '''Kanao 2nd Bridge''' * '''Benten Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 22|Route 22]] * '''Kase Bridge''' * '''Ōkura Bridge''' * '''Takoebashi Bridge''' * '''Shin-Takoebashi Bridge''' – [[Japan National Route 126|Route 126]] * '''JR Sōbu Main Line Bridge''' * '''Rokumanbu Bridge''' * '''Nakadai Bridge''' * '''Sakae Bridge''' * '''Komatsu Bridge''' * '''Orito Bridge''' * '''Kido Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 122|Route 122]] * '''Ryokai Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 30|Route 30]]

revision.comment: 14 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:木戸川 (千葉県) up to rev 102673435
revision.text:
{{otheruses|九十九里地域を流れる木戸川|[[船橋市]]を流れる[[利根川]]水系[[桑納川]]支流の河川|木戸川 (船橋市)}} {{Infobox 河川 |名称 = 木戸川 |画像 = [[画像:Kidoriver2.jpg|300px]] |画像説明 = 木戸橋より下流方 |水系等級 = [[二級水系]] |水系 = 木戸川 |種別 = [[二級河川]] |延長 = 21.5 |標高 = 35 |流量 = |観測所 = |流域面積 = 72.1 |水源 = [[成田市]]本城付近 |河口 = [[太平洋]]（[[山武市]]） |流域 = [[山武市]]・[[山武郡]][[芝山町]]・[[横芝光町]]・[[成田市]] |脚注 = |出典 = [https://www.maff.go.jp/kanto/nouson/sekkei/kokuei/ryoso/shizen/01_2.html] }} [[画像:Kidoriver1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|木戸橋より上流方]] '''木戸川'''（きどがわ）は、[[千葉県]]北東部[[九十九里平野]]中央部を流れる[[二級河川]]。木戸川水系の本流である。
== 地理 == [[成田市]]本城の調整池を水源とし南東流する。[[山武郡]][[芝山町]]との境界を流れたのち町西部を縦貫し、西からいくつかの小支流を合わせる。芝山町を抜けると[[横芝光町]]・[[山武市]]の境界となり、すぐに山武市内へ入る。周辺には[[芝山古墳群]]や山室姫塚古墳などの史跡が存在する。柴原付近で谷津を抜け九十九里平野へ入るが、市街地は少なく水田地帯の中を流れてゆく。両岸は畝が高く、水生植物が発達している。緑海橋を過ぎると[[太平洋]]に注ぐ。周囲は[[九十九里浜]]で、木戸川を境として西側が白幡海水浴場、東側が南浜海水浴場である。流路延長21.5kmのうち、芝山町牧野付近より下流20.9kmが二級河川に指定されている。
当河川は元々定名が無く、旧田越村（現山武市松尾町田越）では田越川、旧[[借毛本郷村]]（現山武市松尾町[[借毛本郷]]）では借毛新井川、旧木戸村（現山武市木戸）では木戸川と称していたが、このうち木戸川の名が広く用いられるようになった。
== 治水 == 木戸川は[[1966年]]（昭和41年）より広域河川改修事業にて、JR総武本線橋梁から小松大橋までの4km区間で河川改修が開始され、拡幅及び堤防の整備がなされた。[[1972年]]（昭和47年）以降は周辺の土地改良事業により小松大橋より下流の区間及び中流域の整備が並行して行われ、[[1997年]]（平成9年）までに全て完了した。以降は[[2009年]]（平成21年）にJR総武本線橋梁上流120mの両岸築堤嵩上げ工事が行われたことを除き休止状態にあった<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305090443/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!11273!310!10?Template=DocOneFrame 山武市議会会議録 平成21年第4回定例会第2日目（12月2日）]</ref>。
[[2011年]]（平成23年）[[3月11日]]の[[東日本大震災]]では、九十九里平野の中央に位置する当河川が周辺河川よりもひときわ大きな被害を受けた。飯岡海岸の防潮堤を乗り越えた津波が8km上流の田越橋付近まで遡上し<ref>[http://wwwp.pref.chiba.lg.jp/pbgikai/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest06!!25275!1!1!318,-1,318!1986!159440!318,-1,318!1986!159440!324,323,322!14!23!144606!65!1?Template=DocOneFrame 千葉県議会会議録 平成24年9月定例会第7日目（10月3日）]</ref>、河口付近の5箇所で堤防が決壊するなど甚大な洪水被害をもたらし、中流部では護岸が崩落するなど整備状況の甘さが露呈する形となった。これを受け同年7月上旬より河口から緑海橋までの733.6mについて査定を受け、堤防を1m嵩上げする工事が行われている。[[2014年]]（平成26年）3月までの予定で、用地買収を要しない区間については[[2012年]]（平成24年）12月に終了している<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame 山武市議会会議録 平成23年第3回定例会第2日目（9月6日）]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame 山武市議会会議録 平成24年第3回定例会第2日目（9月11日）]</ref>。周辺の未利用地を避難道路等に活用する施策も進められている。内陸地域は2009年の築堤嵩上げに引き続き、上流部に向けて進められている。
環境保全に関しては、[[1992年]]（平成4年）5月に「美しい木戸川を守る会」が設立され、以降啓蒙活動や清掃活動が続けられている<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf 成田市の環境平成24年版第2章 環境行政の体制（成田市）]</ref>。
== 主な橋 == * 川津場橋 - [[国道296号]] * 小池橋 - [[千葉県道45号八日市場八街線]] * 明治橋 * 松葉橋 - [[千葉県道116号横芝山武線]] * [[首都圏中央連絡自動車道]] * 古和橋 - [[千葉県道112号成田成東線]] * 古和1号橋 * 古和5号橋 * 古和4号橋 * 金尾1号橋 * 金尾2号橋 * 弁天橋 - [[千葉県道22号千葉八街横芝線]] * 加勢橋 * 大蔵橋 * 田越橋 * 新田越橋 - [[国道126号]] * JR[[総武本線]]橋梁 * 六万部橋 * 中台橋 * 栄橋 * 小松大橋 * 折戸橋 * 木戸橋 - [[千葉県道122号飯岡片貝線]] * 緑海橋 - [[千葉県道30号飯岡一宮線]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考資料 == * [http://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gikan/kikaku/documents/08_02_2.pdf 二級河川木戸川の復旧工事（千葉県・山武土木事務所）] * [http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!17543!63!20?Template=DocOneFrame 山武市議会会議録 平成23年第2回定例会第2日目（6月9日）]
== 関連項目 == * [[千葉県の二級水系一覧]]
{{デフォルトソート:きとかわ}} [[Category:千葉県の河川]] [[Category:千葉県の二級河川]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[木戸川 (千葉県)]] to [[Kido River (Chiba)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{otheruses|九十九里地域を流れる木戸川|[[船橋市]]を流れる[[利根川]]水系[[桑納川]]支流の河川|木戸川 (船橋市)}} {{Infobox 河川 |名称 = 木戸川 |画像 = [[画像:Kidoriver2.jpg|300px]] |画像説明 = 木戸橋より下流方 |水系等級 = [[二級水系]] |水系 = 木戸川 |種別 = [[二級河川]] |延長 = 21.5 |標高 = 35 |流量 = |観測所 = |流域面積 = 72.1 |水源 = [[成田市]]本城付近 |河口 = [[太平洋]]（[[山武市]]） |流域 = [[山武市]]・[[山武郡]][[芝山町]]・[[横芝光町]]・[[成田市]] |脚注 = |出典 = [https://www.maff.go.jp/kanto/nouson/sekkei/kokuei/ryoso/shizen/01_2.html] }} [[画像:Kidoriver1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|木戸橋より上流方]] '''木戸川'''（きどがわ）は、[[千葉県]]北東部[[九十九里平野]]中央部を流れる[[二級河川]]。木戸川水系の本流である。
== 地理 == [[成田市]]本城の調整池を水源とし南東流する。[[山武郡]][[芝山町]]との境界を流れたのち町西部を縦貫し、西からいくつかの小支流を合わせる。芝山町を抜けると[[横芝光町]]・[[山武市]]の境界となり、すぐに山武市内へ入る。周辺には[[芝山古墳群]]や山室姫塚古墳などの史跡が存在する。柴原付近で谷津を抜け九十九里平野へ入るが、市街地は少なく水田地帯の中を流れてゆく。両岸は畝が高く、水生植物が発達している。緑海橋を過ぎると[[太平洋]]に注ぐ。周囲は[[九十九里浜]]で、木戸川を境として西側が白幡海水浴場、東側が南浜海水浴場である。流路延長21.5kmのうち、芝山町牧野付近より下流20.9kmが二級河川に指定されている。
当河川は元々定名が無く、旧田越村（現山武市松尾町田越）では田越川、旧[[借毛本郷村]]（現山武市松尾町[[借毛本郷]]）では借毛新井川、旧木戸村（現山武市木戸）では木戸川と称していたが、このうち木戸川の名が広く用いられるようになった。
== 治水 == 木戸川は[[1966年]]（昭和41年）より広域河川改修事業にて、JR総武本線橋梁から小松大橋までの4km区間で河川改修が開始され、拡幅及び堤防の整備がなされた。[[1972年]]（昭和47年）以降は周辺の土地改良事業により小松大橋より下流の区間及び中流域の整備が並行して行われ、[[1997年]]（平成9年）までに全て完了した。以降は[[2009年]]（平成21年）にJR総武本線橋梁上流120mの両岸築堤嵩上げ工事が行われたことを除き休止状態にあった<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305090443/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!11273!310!10?Template=DocOneFrame 山武市議会会議録 平成21年第4回定例会第2日目（12月2日）]</ref>。
[[2011年]]（平成23年）[[3月11日]]の[[東日本大震災]]では、九十九里平野の中央に位置する当河川が周辺河川よりもひときわ大きな被害を受けた。飯岡海岸の防潮堤を乗り越えた津波が8km上流の田越橋付近まで遡上し<ref>[http://wwwp.pref.chiba.lg.jp/pbgikai/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest06!!25275!1!1!318,-1,318!1986!159440!318,-1,318!1986!159440!324,323,322!14!23!144606!65!1?Template=DocOneFrame 千葉県議会会議録 平成24年9月定例会第7日目（10月3日）]</ref>、河口付近の5箇所で堤防が決壊するなど甚大な洪水被害をもたらし、中流部では護岸が崩落するなど整備状況の甘さが露呈する形となった。これを受け同年7月上旬より河口から緑海橋までの733.6mについて査定を受け、堤防を1m嵩上げする工事が行われている。[[2014年]]（平成26年）3月までの予定で、用地買収を要しない区間については[[2012年]]（平成24年）12月に終了している<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame 山武市議会会議録 平成23年第3回定例会第2日目（9月6日）]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame 山武市議会会議録 平成24年第3回定例会第2日目（9月11日）]</ref>。周辺の未利用地を避難道路等に活用する施策も進められている。内陸地域は2009年の築堤嵩上げに引き続き、上流部に向けて進められている。
環境保全に関しては、[[1992年]]（平成4年）5月に「美しい木戸川を守る会」が設立され、以降啓蒙活動や清掃活動が続けられている<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf 成田市の環境平成24年版第2章 環境行政の体制（成田市）]</ref>。
== 主な橋 == * 川津場橋 - [[国道296号]] * 小池橋 - [[千葉県道45号八日市場八街線]] * 明治橋 * 松葉橋 - [[千葉県道116号横芝山武線]] * [[首都圏中央連絡自動車道]] * 古和橋 - [[千葉県道112号成田成東線]] * 古和1号橋 * 古和5号橋 * 古和4号橋 * 金尾1号橋 * 金尾2号橋 * 弁天橋 - [[千葉県道22号千葉八街横芝線]] * 加勢橋 * 大蔵橋 * 田越橋 * 新田越橋 - [[国道126号]] * JR[[総武本線]]橋梁 * 六万部橋 * 中台橋 * 栄橋 * 小松大橋 * 折戸橋 * 木戸橋 - [[千葉県道122号飯岡片貝線]] * 緑海橋 - [[千葉県道30号飯岡一宮線]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考資料 == * [http://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gikan/kikaku/documents/08_02_2.pdf 二級河川木戸川の復旧工事（千葉県・山武土木事務所）] * [http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!17543!63!20?Template=DocOneFrame 山武市議会会議録 平成23年第2回定例会第2日目（6月9日）]
== 関連項目 == * [[千葉県の二級水系一覧]]

revision.comment: Bot: restore translated version
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Kido River in the Kujūkuri region|the tributary of the Tonegawa system in Funabashi City|Kido River (Funabashi)}} {{Infobox river | name = Kido River | image = [[File:Kidoriver2.jpg|300px]] | image_caption = View downstream from Kido Bridge | watercourse = [[Secondary watercourse (Japan)|Secondary watercourse]] | watercourse_system = Kido River system | type = [[Secondary river]] | length = 21.5 km | elevation = 35 m | discharge = | observation_station = | basin_area = 72.1 km² | source = [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]], Honjō area | mouth = [[Pacific Ocean]] (Sanmu City) | basin = [[Sanmu, Chiba|Sanmu City]], [[Sanbu District, Chiba|Sanbu District]] (Shibayama Town, Yokoshibahikari Town), [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]] | footnotes = | source_url = [https://www.maff.go.jp/kanto/nouson/sekkei/kokuei/ryoso/shizen/01_2.html] }} [[File:Kidoriver1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Upstream view from Kido Bridge]] '''Kido River''' (木戸川, *Kido-gawa*) is a [[secondary river]] flowing through the '''central Kujūkuri Plain''' in '''northeastern Chiba Prefecture, Japan'''. It is the main river of the '''Kido River system'''.
== Geography == The Kido River originates in '''Narita City’s Honjō area''', where it is fed by an '''adjustment pond''', before flowing '''southeast'''. It runs along the '''border of Shibayama Town''' in Sanbu District and then '''passes through the western part of the town''', where it is joined by '''several small tributaries''' from the west.
Exiting Shibayama, the river marks the '''boundary between Yokoshibahikari Town and Sanmu City''' before entering '''Sanmu City'''. Along its course, '''historical sites''' such as the '''Shibayama Kofun Group''' and the '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' can be found. The river then '''exits the valley at Shibahara''', flowing into the '''Kujūkuri Plain''', where it meanders through '''rice fields'''.
Both banks of the river feature '''high ridges''', supporting '''thriving aquatic plant life'''. After passing '''Ryokai Bridge''', the river '''empties into the Pacific Ocean''', bordering '''Kujūkuri Beach'''. The '''Shirahata Beach (west side)''' and '''Minamihama Beach (east side)''' are separated by the river’s mouth.
The river is '''21.5 km long''', with '''20.9 km (from Makino, Shibayama Town, to the mouth) designated as a secondary river'''.
=== Naming === Originally, the Kido River had '''no fixed name'''. Different localities referred to the river by '''various names''': * '''Takoe River''' (*Takoegawa*) in '''Takoe Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Karumo Arai River''' (*Karumo Shingawa*) in '''Karumo Hongō Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Kido River''' (*Kido-gawa*) in '''Kido Village''' (now '''Sanmu City''')
Among these, '''"Kido River"''' became the widely accepted name.
== Flood Control == In '''1966 (Shōwa 41)''', '''a large-scale river improvement project''' was initiated to '''expand and fortify''' the river. The initial phase '''(1966–1972)''' focused on '''4 km from the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge to Komatsu Bridge'''. Later, improvements were made to the '''middle and lower sections''', with the project being completed in '''1997 (Heisei 9)'''.
After Completion tier 6]][[Category:, further work '''remained paused''' except for a '''levee heightening project''' in '''2009''' near the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge.
=== 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake & Tsunami Impact === During the '''2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami''', the Kido River '''sustained significant damage''', surpassing '''all other nearby rivers''' in terms of destruction. The '''tsunami from Iinoura Coast breached the sea wall''' and traveled '''8 km upstream''', reaching '''Takoebashi Bridge'''.<ref>[http://wwwp.pref.chiba.lg.jp/pbgikai/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest06!!25275!1!1!318,-1,318!1986!159440!318,-1,318!1986!159440!324,323,322!14!23!144606!65!1?Template=DocOneFrame Chiba Prefecture Assembly Records – Heisei 24 (2012), 9th Session]</ref>
The '''tsunami destroyed levees in five locations near the river mouth''' and caused '''bank collapses in the middle reaches'''. This '''exposed weaknesses in the flood control system''', leading to a '''1-meter levee reinforcement project''' starting in '''July 2011'''. The '''first phase''' (non-land acquisition areas) was completed in '''December 2012''', while the remaining sections were scheduled for Completion tier 6]][[Category:by '''March 2014'''.<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 23 (2011), 3rd Session, Sept 6]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 24 (2012), 3rd Session, Sept 11]</ref>
A '''proposal for using nearby vacant land as evacuation routes''' was also introduced. Meanwhile, the '''upper course levee reinforcement project''' has continued beyond 2009.
=== Environmental Preservation === The '''"Protect the Beautiful Kido River" Committee''' was founded in '''May 1992''', engaging in '''awareness campaigns and river clean-up efforts'''.<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf "Environmental Report of Narita City – Heisei 24 (2012)"]</ref>
== Major Bridges == * '''Kawazuba Bridge''' – [[Japan National Route 296|Route 296]] * '''Koike Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 45|Route 45]] * '''Meiji Bridge''' * '''Matsuba Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 116|Route 116]] * '''Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway (Ken-Ō Expressway)''' * '''Kowa Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 112|Route 112]] * '''Kowa 1st Bridge''' * '''Kowa 5th Bridge''' * '''Kowa 4th Bridge''' * '''Kanao 1st Bridge''' * '''Kanao 2nd Bridge''' * '''Benten Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 22|Route 22]] * '''Kase Bridge''' * '''Ōkura Bridge''' * '''Takoebashi Bridge''' * '''Shin-Takoebashi Bridge''' – [[Japan National Route 126|Route 126]] * '''JR Sōbu Main Line Bridge''' * '''Rokumanbu Bridge''' * '''Nakadai Bridge''' * '''Sakae Bridge''' * '''Komatsu Bridge''' * '''Orito Bridge''' * '''Kido Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 122|Route 122]] * '''Ryokai Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 30|Route 30]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of secondary rivers in Chiba Prefecture]]
{{citation needed|Kido River}}

After Completion tier 6]][[Category:, further work '''remained paused''' except for a '''levee heightening project''' in '''2009''' near the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge.

The '''tsunami from Iinoura Coast breached the sea wall''' and traveled '''8 km upstream''', reaching '''Takoebashi Bridge'''.<ref>[http://wwwp.pref.chiba.lg.jp/pbgikai/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest06!!25275!1!1!318,-1,318!1986!159440!318,-1,318!1986!159440!324,323,322!14!23!144606!65!1?Template=DocOneFrame Chiba Prefecture Assembly Records – Heisei 24 (2012), 9th Session]</ref>
The '''tsunami destroyed levees in five locations near the river mouth''' and caused '''bank collapses in the middle reaches'''. This '''exposed weaknesses in the flood control system''', leading to a '''1-meter levee reinforcement project''' starting in '''July 2011'''. The '''first phase''' (non-land acquisition areas) was completed in '''December 2012''', while the remaining sections were scheduled for Completion tier 6]][[Category:by '''March 2014'''.<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 23 (2011), 3rd Session, Sept 6]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 24 (2012), 3rd Session, Sept 11]</ref>

After Completion tier 6]][[Category:, further work '''remained paused''' except for a '''levee heightening project''' in '''2009''' near the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge.
=== 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake & Tsunami Impact === During the '''2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami''', the Kido River '''sustained significant damage''', surpassing '''all other nearby rivers''' in terms of destruction. The '''tsunami from Iinoura Coast breached the sea wall''' and traveled '''8 km upstream''', reaching '''Takoebashi Bridge'''.<ref>[http://wwwp.pref.chiba.lg.jp/pbgikai/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest06!!25275!1!1!318,-1,318!1986!159440!318,-1,318!1986!159440!324,323,322!14!23!144606!65!1?Template=DocOneFrame Chiba Prefecture Assembly Records – Heisei 24 (2012), 9th Session]</ref>
The '''tsunami destroyed levees in five locations near the river mouth''' and caused '''bank collapses in the middle reaches'''. This '''exposed weaknesses in the flood control system''', leading to a '''1-meter levee reinforcement project''' starting in '''July 2011'''. The '''first phase''' (non-land acquisition areas) was completed in '''December 2012''', while the remaining sections were scheduled for Completion tier 6]][[Category:by '''March 2014'''.<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 23 (2011), 3rd Session, Sept 6]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 24 (2012), 3rd Session, Sept 11]</ref>
A '''proposal for using nearby vacant land as evacuation routes''' was also introduced. Meanwhile, the '''upper course levee reinforcement project''' has continued beyond 2009.
=== Environmental Preservation === The '''"Protect the Beautiful Kido River" Committee''' was founded in '''May 1992''', engaging in '''awareness campaigns and river clean-up efforts'''.<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf "Environmental Report of Narita City – Heisei 24 (2012)"]</ref>
== Major Bridges == * '''Kawazuba Bridge''' – [[Japan National Route 296|Route 296]] * '''Koike Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 45|Route 45]] * '''Meiji Bridge''' * '''Matsuba Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 116|Route 116]] * '''Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway (Ken-Ō Expressway)''' * '''Kowa Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 112|Route 112]] * '''Kowa 1st Bridge''' * '''Kowa 5th Bridge''' * '''Kowa 4th Bridge''' * '''Kanao 1st Bridge''' * '''Kanao 2nd Bridge''' * '''Benten Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 22|Route 22]] * '''Kase Bridge''' * '''Ōkura Bridge''' * '''Takoebashi Bridge''' * '''Shin-Takoebashi Bridge''' – [[Japan National Route 126|Route 126]] * '''JR Sōbu Main Line Bridge''' * '''Rokumanbu Bridge''' * '''Nakadai Bridge''' * '''Sakae Bridge''' * '''Komatsu Bridge''' * '''Orito Bridge''' * '''Kido Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 122|Route 122]] * '''Ryokai Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 30|Route 30]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Kido River in the Kujūkuri region|the tributary of the Tonegawa system in Funabashi City|Kido River (Funabashi)}} {{Infobox river | name = Kido River | image = [[File:Kidoriver2.jpg|300px]] | image_caption = View downstream from Kido Bridge | watercourse = [[Secondary watercourse (Japan)|Secondary watercourse]] | watercourse_system = Kido River system | type = [[Secondary river]] | length = 21.5 km | elevation = 35 m | discharge = | observation_station = | basin_area = 72.1 km² | source = [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]], Honjō area | mouth = [[Pacific Ocean]] (Sanmu City) | basin = [[Sanmu, Chiba|Sanmu City]], [[Sanbu District, Chiba|Sanbu District]] (Shibayama Town, Yokoshibahikari Town), [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]] | footnotes = | source_url = [https://www.maff.go.jp/kanto/nouson/sekkei/kokuei/ryoso/shizen/01_2.html] }} [[File:Kidoriver1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Upstream view from Kido Bridge]] '''Kido River''' (木戸川, *Kido-gawa*) is a [[secondary river]] flowing through the '''central Kujūkuri Plain''' in '''northeastern Chiba Prefecture, Japan'''. It is the main river of the '''Kido River system'''.
== Geography == The Kido River originates in '''Narita City’s Honjō area''', where it is fed by an '''adjustment pond''', before flowing '''southeast'''. It runs along the '''border of Shibayama Town''' in Sanbu District and then '''passes through the western part of the town''', where it is joined by '''several small tributaries''' from the west.
Exiting Shibayama, the river marks the '''boundary between Yokoshibahikari Town and Sanmu City''' before entering '''Sanmu City'''. Along its course, '''historical sites''' such as the '''Shibayama Kofun Group''' and the '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' can be found. The river then '''exits the valley at Shibahara''', flowing into the '''Kujūkuri Plain''', where it meanders through '''rice fields'''.
Both banks of the river feature '''high ridges''', supporting '''thriving aquatic plant life'''. After passing '''Ryokai Bridge''', the river '''empties into the Pacific Ocean''', bordering '''Kujūkuri Beach'''. The '''Shirahata Beach (west side)''' and '''Minamihama Beach (east side)''' are separated by the river’s mouth.
The river is '''21.5 km long''', with '''20.9 km (from Makino, Shibayama Town, to the mouth) designated as a secondary river'''.
=== Naming === Originally, the Kido River had '''no fixed name'''. Different localities referred to the river by '''various names''': * '''Takoe River''' (*Takoegawa*) in '''Takoe Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Karumo Arai River''' (*Karumo Shingawa*) in '''Karumo Hongō Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Kido River''' (*Kido-gawa*) in '''Kido Village''' (now '''Sanmu City''')
Among these, '''"Kido River"''' became the widely accepted name.
== Flood Control == In '''1966 (Shōwa 41)''', '''a large-scale river improvement project''' was initiated to '''expand and fortify''' the river. The initial phase '''(1966–1972)''' focused on '''4 km from the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge to Komatsu Bridge'''. Later, improvements were made to the '''middle and lower sections''', with the project being completed in '''1997 (Heisei 9)'''.
After Completion tier 6]][[Category:, further work '''remained paused''' except for a '''levee heightening project''' in '''2009''' near the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge.
=== 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake & Tsunami Impact === During the '''2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami''', the Kido River '''sustained significant damage''', surpassing '''all other nearby rivers''' in terms of destruction. The '''tsunami from Iinoura Coast breached the sea wall''' and traveled '''8 km upstream''', reaching '''Takoebashi Bridge'''.<ref>[http://wwwp.pref.chiba.lg.jp/pbgikai/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest06!!25275!1!1!318,-1,318!1986!159440!318,-1,318!1986!159440!324,323,322!14!23!144606!65!1?Template=DocOneFrame Chiba Prefecture Assembly Records – Heisei 24 (2012), 9th Session]</ref>
The '''tsunami destroyed levees in five locations near the river mouth''' and caused '''bank collapses in the middle reaches'''. This '''exposed weaknesses in the flood control system''', leading to a '''1-meter levee reinforcement project''' starting in '''July 2011'''. The '''first phase''' (non-land acquisition areas) was completed in '''December 2012''', while the remaining sections were scheduled for Completion tier 6]][[Category:by '''March 2014'''.<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 23 (2011), 3rd Session, Sept 6]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 24 (2012), 3rd Session, Sept 11]</ref>
A '''proposal for using nearby vacant land as evacuation routes''' was also introduced. Meanwhile, the '''upper course levee reinforcement project''' has continued beyond 2009.
=== Environmental Preservation === The '''"Protect the Beautiful Kido River" Committee''' was founded in '''May 1992''', engaging in '''awareness campaigns and river clean-up efforts'''.<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf "Environmental Report of Narita City – Heisei 24 (2012)"]</ref>
== Major Bridges == * '''Kawazuba Bridge''' – [[Japan National Route 296|Route 296]] * '''Koike Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 45|Route 45]] * '''Meiji Bridge''' * '''Matsuba Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 116|Route 116]] * '''Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway (Ken-Ō Expressway)''' * '''Kowa Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 112|Route 112]] * '''Kowa 1st Bridge''' * '''Kowa 5th Bridge''' * '''Kowa 4th Bridge''' * '''Kanao 1st Bridge''' * '''Kanao 2nd Bridge''' * '''Benten Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 22|Route 22]] * '''Kase Bridge''' * '''Ōkura Bridge''' * '''Takoebashi Bridge''' * '''Shin-Takoebashi Bridge''' – [[Japan National Route 126|Route 126]] * '''JR Sōbu Main Line Bridge''' * '''Rokumanbu Bridge''' * '''Nakadai Bridge''' * '''Sakae Bridge''' * '''Komatsu Bridge''' * '''Orito Bridge''' * '''Kido Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 122|Route 122]] * '''Ryokai Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 30|Route 30]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of secondary rivers in Chiba Prefecture]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Kido River in the Kujūkuri region|the tributary of the Tonegawa system in Funabashi City|Kido River (Funabashi)}} {{Infobox river | name = Kido River | image = [[File:Kidoriver2.jpg|300px]] | image_caption = View downstream from Kido Bridge | watercourse = [[Secondary watercourse (Japan)|Secondary watercourse]] | watercourse_system = Kido River system | type = [[Secondary river]] | length = 21.5 km | elevation = 35 m | discharge = | observation_station = | basin_area = 72.1 km² | source = [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]], Honjō area | mouth = [[Pacific Ocean]] (Sanmu City) | basin = [[Sanmu, Chiba|Sanmu City]], [[Sanbu District, Chiba|Sanbu District]] (Shibayama Town, Yokoshibahikari Town), [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]] | footnotes = | source_url = [https://www.maff.go.jp/kanto/nouson/sekkei/kokuei/ryoso/shizen/01_2.html] }} [[File:Kidoriver1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Upstream view from Kido Bridge]] '''Kido River''' (木戸川, *Kido-gawa*) is a [[secondary river]] flowing through the '''central Kujūkuri Plain''' in '''northeastern Chiba Prefecture, Japan'''. It is the main river of the '''Kido River system'''.
== Geography == The Kido River originates in '''Narita City’s Honjō area''', where it is fed by an '''adjustment pond''', before flowing '''southeast'''. It runs along the '''border of Shibayama Town''' in Sanbu District and then '''passes through the western part of the town''', where it is joined by '''several small tributaries''' from the west.
Exiting Shibayama, the river marks the '''boundary between Yokoshibahikari Town and Sanmu City''' before entering '''Sanmu City'''. Along its course, '''historical sites''' such as the '''Shibayama Kofun Group''' and the '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' can be found. The river then '''exits the valley at Shibahara''', flowing into the '''Kujūkuri Plain''', where it meanders through '''rice fields'''.
Both banks of the river feature '''high ridges''', supporting '''thriving aquatic plant life'''. After passing '''Ryokai Bridge''', the river '''empties into the Pacific Ocean''', bordering '''Kujūkuri Beach'''. The '''Shirahata Beach (west side)''' and '''Minamihama Beach (east side)''' are separated by the river’s mouth.
The river is '''21.5 km long''', with '''20.9 km (from Makino, Shibayama Town, to the mouth) designated as a secondary river'''.
=== Naming === Originally, the Kido River had '''no fixed name'''. Different localities referred to the river by '''various names''': * '''Takoe River''' (*Takoegawa*) in '''Takoe Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Karumo Arai River''' (*Karumo Shingawa*) in '''Karumo Hongō Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Kido River''' (*Kido-gawa*) in '''Kido Village''' (now '''Sanmu City''')
Among these, '''"Kido River"''' became the widely accepted name.
== Flood Control == In '''1966 (Shōwa 41)''', '''a large-scale river improvement project''' was initiated to '''expand and fortify''' the river. The initial phase '''(1966–1972)''' focused on '''4 km from the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge to Komatsu Bridge'''. Later, improvements were made to the '''middle and lower sections''', with the project being completed in '''1997 (Heisei 9)'''.
After Completion tier 6]][[Category:, further work '''remained paused''' except for a '''levee heightening project''' in '''2009''' near the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge.
=== 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake & Tsunami Impact === During the '''2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami''', the Kido River '''sustained significant damage''', surpassing '''all other nearby rivers''' in terms of destruction. The '''tsunami from Iinoura Coast breached the sea wall''' and traveled '''8 km upstream''', reaching '''Takoebashi Bridge'''.<ref>[http://wwwp.pref.chiba.lg.jp/pbgikai/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest06!!25275!1!1!318,-1,318!1986!159440!318,-1,318!1986!159440!324,323,322!14!23!144606!65!1?Template=DocOneFrame Chiba Prefecture Assembly Records – Heisei 24 (2012), 9th Session]</ref>
The '''tsunami destroyed levees in five locations near the river mouth''' and caused '''bank collapses in the middle reaches'''. This '''exposed weaknesses in the flood control system''', leading to a '''1-meter levee reinforcement project''' starting in '''July 2011'''. The '''first phase''' (non-land acquisition areas) was completed in '''December 2012''', while the remaining sections were scheduled for Completion tier 6]][[Category:by '''March 2014'''.<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 23 (2011), 3rd Session, Sept 6]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 24 (2012), 3rd Session, Sept 11]</ref>
A '''proposal for using nearby vacant land as evacuation routes''' was also introduced. Meanwhile, the '''upper course levee reinforcement project''' has continued beyond 2009.
=== Environmental Preservation === The '''"Protect the Beautiful Kido River" Committee''' was founded in '''May 1992''', engaging in '''awareness campaigns and river clean-up efforts'''.<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf "Environmental Report of Narita City – Heisei 24 (2012)"]</ref>
== Major Bridges == * '''Kawazuba Bridge''' – [[Japan National Route 296|Route 296]] * '''Koike Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 45|Route 45]] * '''Meiji Bridge''' * '''Matsuba Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 116|Route 116]] * '''Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway (Ken-Ō Expressway)''' * '''Kowa Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 112|Route 112]] * '''Kowa 1st Bridge''' * '''Kowa 5th Bridge''' * '''Kowa 4th Bridge''' * '''Kanao 1st Bridge''' * '''Kanao 2nd Bridge''' * '''Benten Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 22|Route 22]] * '''Kase Bridge''' * '''Ōkura Bridge''' * '''Takoebashi Bridge''' * '''Shin-Takoebashi Bridge''' – [[Japan National Route 126|Route 126]] * '''JR Sōbu Main Line Bridge''' * '''Rokumanbu Bridge''' * '''Nakadai Bridge''' * '''Sakae Bridge''' * '''Komatsu Bridge''' * '''Orito Bridge''' * '''Kido Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 122|Route 122]] * '''Ryokai Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 30|Route 30]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of secondary rivers in Chiba Prefecture]]
{{translated page|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)|version=102673435|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Completion tier 6]] }}
