The '''first phase''' (non-land acquisition areas) was completed in '''December 2012''', while the remaining sections were scheduled for Completion tier 6]][[Category:by '''March 2014'''.<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 23 (2011), 3rd Session, Sept 6]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 24 (2012), 3rd Session, Sept 11]</ref>
A '''proposal for using nearby vacant land as evacuation routes''' was also introduced. Meanwhile, the '''upper course levee reinforcement project''' has continued beyond 2009.
=== Environmental Preservation === The '''"Protect the Beautiful Kido River" Committee''' was founded in '''May 1992''', engaging in '''awareness campaigns and river clean-up efforts'''.<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf "Environmental Report of Narita City – Heisei 24 (2012)"]</ref>
== Major Bridges == * '''Kawazuba Bridge''' – [[:en:Japan National Route 296|Route 296]] * '''Koike Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 45|Route 45]] * '''Meiji Bridge''' * '''Matsuba Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 116|Route 116]] * '''Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway (Ken-Ō Expressway)''' * '''Kowa Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 112|Route 112]] * '''Kowa 1st Bridge''' * '''Kowa 5th Bridge''' * '''Kowa 4th Bridge''' * '''Kanao 1st Bridge''' * '''Kanao 2nd Bridge''' * '''Benten Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 22|Route 22]] * '''Kase Bridge''' * '''Ōkura Bridge''' * '''Takoebashi Bridge''' * '''Shin-Takoebashi Bridge''' – [[:en:Japan National Route 126|Route 126]] * '''JR Sōbu Main Line Bridge''' * '''Rokumanbu Bridge''' * '''Nakadai Bridge''' * '''Sakae Bridge''' * '''Komatsu Bridge''' * '''Orito Bridge''' * '''Kido Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 122|Route 122]] * '''Ryokai Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 30|Route 30]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Kido River in the Kujūkuri region|the tributary of the Tonegawa system in Funabashi City|Kido River (Funabashi)}} {{Infobox river | name = Kido River | image = [[File:Kidoriver2.jpg|300px]] | image_caption = View downstream from Kido Bridge | watercourse = [[Secondary watercourse (Japan)|Secondary watercourse]] | watercourse_system = Kido River system | type = [[Secondary river]] | length = 21.5 km | elevation = 35 m | discharge = | observation_station = | basin_area = 72.1 km² | source = [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]], Honjō area | mouth = [[Pacific Ocean]] (Sanmu City) | basin = [[Sanmu, Chiba|Sanmu City]], [[Sanbu District, Chiba|Sanbu District]] (Shibayama Town, Yokoshibahikari Town), [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]] | footnotes = | source_url = [https://www.maff.go.jp/kanto/nouson/sekkei/kokuei/ryoso/shizen/01_2.html] }} [[File:Kidoriver1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Upstream view from Kido Bridge]] '''Kido River''' (木戸川, *Kido-gawa*) is a [[secondary river]] flowing through the '''central Kujūkuri Plain''' in '''northeastern Chiba Prefecture, Japan'''. It is the main river of the '''Kido River system'''.
== Geography == The Kido River originates in '''Narita City’s Honjō area''', where it is fed by an '''adjustment pond''', before flowing '''southeast'''. It runs along the '''border of Shibayama Town''' in Sanbu District and then '''passes through the western part of the town''', where it is joined by '''several small tributaries''' from the west.
Exiting Shibayama, the river marks the '''boundary between Yokoshibahikari Town and Sanmu City''' before entering '''Sanmu City'''. Along its course, '''historical sites''' such as the '''Shibayama Kofun Group''' and the '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' can be found. The river then '''exits the valley at Shibahara''', flowing into the '''Kujūkuri Plain''', where it meanders through '''rice fields'''.
Both banks of the river feature '''high ridges''', supporting '''thriving aquatic plant life'''. After passing '''Ryokai Bridge''', the river '''empties into the Pacific Ocean''', bordering '''Kujūkuri Beach'''. The '''Shirahata Beach (west side)''' and '''Minamihama Beach (east side)''' are separated by the river’s mouth.
The river is '''21.5 km long''', with '''20.9 km (from Makino, Shibayama Town, to the mouth) designated as a secondary river'''.
=== Naming === Originally, the Kido River had '''no fixed name'''. Different localities referred to the river by '''various names''': * '''Takoe River''' (*Takoegawa*) in '''Takoe Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Karumo Arai River''' (*Karumo Shingawa*) in '''Karumo Hongō Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Kido River''' (*Kido-gawa*) in '''Kido Village''' (now '''Sanmu City''')
Among these, '''"Kido River"''' became the widely accepted name.
== Flood Control == In '''1966 (Shōwa 41)''', '''a large-scale river improvement project''' was initiated to '''expand and fortify''' the river. The initial phase '''(1966–1972)''' focused on '''4 km from the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge to Komatsu Bridge'''. Later, improvements were made to the '''middle and lower sections''', with the project being completed in '''1997 (Heisei 9)'''.
After Completion tier 6]][[Category:, further work '''remained paused''' except for a '''levee heightening project''' in '''2009''' near the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge.
=== 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake & Tsunami Impact === During the '''2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami''', the Kido River '''sustained significant damage''', surpassing '''all other nearby rivers''' in terms of destruction. The '''tsunami from Iinoura Coast breached the sea wall''' and traveled '''8 km upstream''', reaching '''Takoebashi Bridge'''.<ref>[http://wwwp.pref.chiba.lg.jp/pbgikai/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest06!!25275!1!1!318,-1,318!1986!159440!318,-1,318!1986!159440!324,323,322!14!23!144606!65!1?Template=DocOneFrame Chiba Prefecture Assembly Records – Heisei 24 (2012), 9th Session]</ref>
The '''tsunami destroyed levees in five locations near the river mouth''' and caused '''bank collapses in the middle reaches'''. This '''exposed weaknesses in the flood control system''', leading to a '''1-meter levee reinforcement project''' starting in '''July 2011'''. The '''first phase''' (non-land acquisition areas) was completed in '''December 2012''', while the remaining sections were scheduled for Completion tier 6]][[Category:by '''March 2014'''.<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 23 (2011), 3rd Session, Sept 6]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 24 (2012), 3rd Session, Sept 11]</ref>
A '''proposal for using nearby vacant land as evacuation routes''' was also introduced. Meanwhile, the '''upper course levee reinforcement project''' has continued beyond 2009.
=== Environmental Preservation === The '''"Protect the Beautiful Kido River" Committee''' was founded in '''May 1992''', engaging in '''awareness campaigns and river clean-up efforts'''.<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf "Environmental Report of Narita City – Heisei 24 (2012)"]</ref>
== Major Bridges == * '''Kawazuba Bridge''' – [[:en:Japan National Route 296|Route 296]] * '''Koike Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 45|Route 45]] * '''Meiji Bridge''' * '''Matsuba Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 116|Route 116]] * '''Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway (Ken-Ō Expressway)''' * '''Kowa Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 112|Route 112]] * '''Kowa 1st Bridge''' * '''Kowa 5th Bridge''' * '''Kowa 4th Bridge''' * '''Kanao 1st Bridge''' * '''Kanao 2nd Bridge''' * '''Benten Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 22|Route 22]] * '''Kase Bridge''' * '''Ōkura Bridge''' * '''Takoebashi Bridge''' * '''Shin-Takoebashi Bridge''' – [[:en:Japan National Route 126|Route 126]] * '''JR Sōbu Main Line Bridge''' * '''Rokumanbu Bridge''' * '''Nakadai Bridge''' * '''Sakae Bridge''' * '''Komatsu Bridge''' * '''Orito Bridge''' * '''Kido Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 122|Route 122]] * '''Ryokai Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 30|Route 30]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of secondary rivers in Chiba Prefecture]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|Kido River in the Kujūkuri region|the tributary of the Tonegawa system in Funabashi City|Kido River (Funabashi)}} {{Infobox river | name = Kido River | image = [[File:Kidoriver2.jpg|300px]] | image_caption = View downstream from Kido Bridge | watercourse = [[Secondary watercourse (Japan)|Secondary watercourse]] | watercourse_system = Kido River system | type = [[Secondary river]] | length = 21.5 km | elevation = 35 m | discharge = | observation_station = | basin_area = 72.1 km² | source = [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]], Honjō area | mouth = [[:en:Pacific Ocean|Pacific Ocean]] (Sanmu City) | basin = [[Sanmu, Chiba|Sanmu City]], [[Sanbu District, Chiba|Sanbu District]] (Shibayama Town, Yokoshibahikari Town), [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]] | footnotes = | source_url = [https://www.maff.go.jp/kanto/nouson/sekkei/kokuei/ryoso/shizen/01_2.html] }} [[File:Kidoriver1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Upstream view from Kido Bridge]] '''Kido River''' (木戸川, *Kido-gawa*) is a [[secondary river]] flowing through the '''central Kujūkuri Plain''' in '''northeastern Chiba Prefecture, Japan'''. It is the main river of the '''Kido River system'''.
== Geography == The Kido River originates in '''Narita City’s Honjō area''', where it is fed by an '''adjustment pond''', before flowing '''southeast'''. It runs along the '''border of Shibayama Town''' in Sanbu District and then '''passes through the western part of the town''', where it is joined by '''several small tributaries''' from the west.
Exiting Shibayama, the river marks the '''boundary between Yokoshibahikari Town and Sanmu City''' before entering '''Sanmu City'''. Along its course, '''historical sites''' such as the '''Shibayama Kofun Group''' and the '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' can be found. The river then '''exits the valley at Shibahara''', flowing into the '''Kujūkuri Plain''', where it meanders through '''rice fields'''.
Both banks of the river feature '''high ridges''', supporting '''thriving aquatic plant life'''. After passing '''Ryokai Bridge''', the river '''empties into the Pacific Ocean''', bordering '''Kujūkuri Beach'''. The '''Shirahata Beach (west side)''' and '''Minamihama Beach (east side)''' are separated by the river’s mouth.
The river is '''21.5 km long''', with '''20.9 km (from Makino, Shibayama Town, to the mouth) designated as a secondary river'''.
=== Naming === Originally, the Kido River had '''no fixed name'''. Different localities referred to the river by '''various names''': * '''Takoe River''' (*Takoegawa*) in '''Takoe Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Karumo Arai River''' (*Karumo Shingawa*) in '''Karumo Hongō Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Kido River''' (*Kido-gawa*) in '''Kido Village''' (now '''Sanmu City''')
Among these, '''"Kido River"''' became the widely accepted name.
== Flood Control == In '''1966 (Shōwa 41)''', '''a large-scale river improvement project''' was initiated to '''expand and fortify''' the river. The initial phase '''(1966–1972)''' focused on '''4 km from the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge to Komatsu Bridge'''. Later, improvements were made to the '''middle and lower sections''', with the project being completed in '''1997 (Heisei 9)'''.
After Completion tier 6]][[Category:, further work '''remained paused''' except for a '''levee heightening project''' in '''2009''' near the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge.
=== 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake & Tsunami Impact === During the '''2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami''', the Kido River '''sustained significant damage''', surpassing '''all other nearby rivers''' in terms of destruction. The '''tsunami from Iinoura Coast breached the sea wall''' and traveled '''8 km upstream''', reaching '''Takoebashi Bridge'''.<ref>[http://wwwp.pref.chiba.lg.jp/pbgikai/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest06!!25275!1!1!318,-1,318!1986!159440!318,-1,318!1986!159440!324,323,322!14!23!144606!65!1?Template=DocOneFrame Chiba Prefecture Assembly Records – Heisei 24 (2012), 9th Session]</ref>
The '''tsunami destroyed levees in five locations near the river mouth''' and caused '''bank collapses in the middle reaches'''. This '''exposed weaknesses in the flood control system''', leading to a '''1-meter levee reinforcement project''' starting in '''July 2011'''. The '''first phase''' (non-land acquisition areas) was completed in '''December 2012''', while the remaining sections were scheduled for Completion tier 6]][[Category:by '''March 2014'''.<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 23 (2011), 3rd Session, Sept 6]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 24 (2012), 3rd Session, Sept 11]</ref>
A '''proposal for using nearby vacant land as evacuation routes''' was also introduced. Meanwhile, the '''upper course levee reinforcement project''' has continued beyond 2009.
=== Environmental Preservation === The '''"Protect the Beautiful Kido River" Committee''' was founded in '''May 1992''', engaging in '''awareness campaigns and river clean-up efforts'''.<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf "Environmental Report of Narita City – Heisei 24 (2012)"]</ref>
== Major Bridges == * '''Kawazuba Bridge''' – [[:en:Japan National Route 296|Route 296]] * '''Koike Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 45|Route 45]] * '''Meiji Bridge''' * '''Matsuba Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 116|Route 116]] * '''Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway (Ken-Ō Expressway)''' * '''Kowa Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 112|Route 112]] * '''Kowa 1st Bridge''' * '''Kowa 5th Bridge''' * '''Kowa 4th Bridge''' * '''Kanao 1st Bridge''' * '''Kanao 2nd Bridge''' * '''Benten Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 22|Route 22]] * '''Kase Bridge''' * '''Ōkura Bridge''' * '''Takoebashi Bridge''' * '''Shin-Takoebashi Bridge''' – [[:en:Japan National Route 126|Route 126]] * '''JR Sōbu Main Line Bridge''' * '''Rokumanbu Bridge''' * '''Nakadai Bridge''' * '''Sakae Bridge''' * '''Komatsu Bridge''' * '''Orito Bridge''' * '''Kido Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 122|Route 122]] * '''Ryokai Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 30|Route 30]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of secondary rivers in Chiba Prefecture]]

After Completion tier 6]][[Category:, further work '''remained paused''' except for a '''levee heightening project''' in '''2009''' near the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge.

Different localities referred to the river by '''various names''': * '''Takoe River''' (*Takoegawa*) in '''Takoe Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Karumo Arai River''' (*Karumo Shingawa*) in '''Karumo Hongō Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Kido River''' (*Kido-gawa*) in '''Kido Village''' (now '''Sanmu City''')
Among these, '''"Kido River"''' became the widely accepted name.
== Flood Control == In '''1966 (Shōwa 41)''', '''a large-scale river improvement project''' was initiated to '''expand and fortify''' the river. The initial phase '''(1966–1972)''' focused on '''4 km from the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge to Komatsu Bridge'''. Later, improvements were made to the '''middle and lower sections''', with the project being completed in '''1997 (Heisei 9)'''.
After Completion tier 6]][[Category:, further work '''remained paused''' except for a '''levee heightening project''' in '''2009''' near the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge.
=== 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake & Tsunami Impact === During the '''2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami''', the Kido River '''sustained significant damage''', surpassing '''all other nearby rivers''' in terms of destruction. The '''tsunami from Iinoura Coast breached the sea wall''' and traveled '''8 km upstream''', reaching '''Takoebashi Bridge'''.<ref>[http://wwwp.pref.chiba.lg.jp/pbgikai/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest06!!25275!1!1!318,-1,318!1986!159440!318,-1,318!1986!159440!324,323,322!14!23!144606!65!1?Template=DocOneFrame Chiba Prefecture Assembly Records – Heisei 24 (2012), 9th Session]</ref>
The '''tsunami destroyed levees in five locations near the river mouth''' and caused '''bank collapses in the middle reaches'''. This '''exposed weaknesses in the flood control system''', leading to a '''1-meter levee reinforcement project''' starting in '''July 2011'''. The '''first phase''' (non-land acquisition areas) was completed in '''December 2012''', while the remaining sections were scheduled for Completion tier 6]][[Category:by '''March 2014'''.<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 23 (2011), 3rd Session, Sept 6]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 24 (2012), 3rd Session, Sept 11]</ref>
A '''proposal for using nearby vacant land as evacuation routes''' was also introduced. Meanwhile, the '''upper course levee reinforcement project''' has continued beyond 2009.
=== Environmental Preservation === The '''"Protect the Beautiful Kido River" Committee''' was founded in '''May 1992''', engaging in '''awareness campaigns and river clean-up efforts'''.<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf "Environmental Report of Narita City – Heisei 24 (2012)"]</ref>
== Major Bridges == * '''Kawazuba Bridge''' – [[:en:Japan National Route 296|Route 296]] * '''Koike Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 45|Route 45]] * '''Meiji Bridge''' * '''Matsuba Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 116|Route 116]] * '''Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway (Ken-Ō Expressway)''' * '''Kowa Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 112|Route 112]] * '''Kowa 1st Bridge''' * '''Kowa 5th Bridge''' * '''Kowa 4th Bridge''' * '''Kanao 1st Bridge''' * '''Kanao 2nd Bridge''' * '''Benten Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 22|Route 22]] * '''Kase Bridge''' * '''Ōkura Bridge''' * '''Takoebashi Bridge''' * '''Shin-Takoebashi Bridge''' – [[:en:Japan National Route 126|Route 126]] * '''JR Sōbu Main Line Bridge''' * '''Rokumanbu Bridge''' * '''Nakadai Bridge''' * '''Sakae Bridge''' * '''Komatsu Bridge''' * '''Orito Bridge''' * '''Kido Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 122|Route 122]] * '''Ryokai Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 30|Route 30]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of secondary rivers in Chiba Prefecture]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11518415]] Wikidata name: Kido River [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:18, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|Kido River in the Kujūkuri region|the tributary of the Tonegawa system in Funabashi City|Kido River (Funabashi)}} {{Infobox river | name = Kido River | image = [[File:Kidoriver2.jpg|300px]] | image_caption = View downstream from Kido Bridge | watercourse = [[Secondary watercourse (Japan)|Secondary watercourse]] | watercourse_system = Kido River system | type = [[Secondary river]] | length = 21.5 km | elevation = 35 m | discharge = | observation_station = | basin_area = 72.1 km² | source = [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]], Honjō area | mouth = [[:en:Pacific Ocean|Pacific Ocean]] (Sanmu City) | basin = [[Sanmu, Chiba|Sanmu City]], [[Sanbu District, Chiba|Sanbu District]] (Shibayama Town, Yokoshibahikari Town), [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]] | footnotes = | source_url = [https://www.maff.go.jp/kanto/nouson/sekkei/kokuei/ryoso/shizen/01_2.html] }} [[File:Kidoriver1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Upstream view from Kido Bridge]] '''Kido River''' (木戸川, *Kido-gawa*) is a [[secondary river]] flowing through the '''central Kujūkuri Plain''' in '''northeastern Chiba Prefecture, Japan'''. It is the main river of the '''Kido River system'''.
== Geography == The Kido River originates in '''Narita City’s Honjō area''', where it is fed by an '''adjustment pond''', before flowing '''southeast'''. It runs along the '''border of Shibayama Town''' in Sanbu District and then '''passes through the western part of the town''', where it is joined by '''several small tributaries''' from the west.
Exiting Shibayama, the river marks the '''boundary between Yokoshibahikari Town and Sanmu City''' before entering '''Sanmu City'''. Along its course, '''historical sites''' such as the '''Shibayama Kofun Group''' and the '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' can be found. The river then '''exits the valley at Shibahara''', flowing into the '''Kujūkuri Plain''', where it meanders through '''rice fields'''.
Both banks of the river feature '''high ridges''', supporting '''thriving aquatic plant life'''. After passing '''Ryokai Bridge''', the river '''empties into the Pacific Ocean''', bordering '''Kujūkuri Beach'''. The '''Shirahata Beach (west side)''' and '''Minamihama Beach (east side)''' are separated by the river’s mouth.
The river is '''21.5 km long''', with '''20.9 km (from Makino, Shibayama Town, to the mouth) designated as a secondary river'''.
=== Naming === Originally, the Kido River had '''no fixed name'''. Different localities referred to the river by '''various names''': * '''Takoe River''' (*Takoegawa*) in '''Takoe Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Karumo Arai River''' (*Karumo Shingawa*) in '''Karumo Hongō Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Kido River''' (*Kido-gawa*) in '''Kido Village''' (now '''Sanmu City''')
Among these, '''"Kido River"''' became the widely accepted name.
== Flood Control == In '''1966 (Shōwa 41)''', '''a large-scale river improvement project''' was initiated to '''expand and fortify''' the river. The initial phase '''(1966–1972)''' focused on '''4 km from the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge to Komatsu Bridge'''. Later, improvements were made to the '''middle and lower sections''', with the project being completed in '''1997 (Heisei 9)'''.
After Completion tier 6]][[Category:, further work '''remained paused''' except for a '''levee heightening project''' in '''2009''' near the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge.
=== 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake & Tsunami Impact === During the '''2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami''', the Kido River '''sustained significant damage''', surpassing '''all other nearby rivers''' in terms of destruction. The '''tsunami from Iinoura Coast breached the sea wall''' and traveled '''8 km upstream''', reaching '''Takoebashi Bridge'''.<ref>[http://wwwp.pref.chiba.lg.jp/pbgikai/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest06!!25275!1!1!318,-1,318!1986!159440!318,-1,318!1986!159440!324,323,322!14!23!144606!65!1?Template=DocOneFrame Chiba Prefecture Assembly Records – Heisei 24 (2012), 9th Session]</ref>
The '''tsunami destroyed levees in five locations near the river mouth''' and caused '''bank collapses in the middle reaches'''. This '''exposed weaknesses in the flood control system''', leading to a '''1-meter levee reinforcement project''' starting in '''July 2011'''. The '''first phase''' (non-land acquisition areas) was completed in '''December 2012''', while the remaining sections were scheduled for Completion tier 6]][[Category:by '''March 2014'''.<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 23 (2011), 3rd Session, Sept 6]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 24 (2012), 3rd Session, Sept 11]</ref>
A '''proposal for using nearby vacant land as evacuation routes''' was also introduced. Meanwhile, the '''upper course levee reinforcement project''' has continued beyond 2009.
=== Environmental Preservation === The '''"Protect the Beautiful Kido River" Committee''' was founded in '''May 1992''', engaging in '''awareness campaigns and river clean-up efforts'''.<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf "Environmental Report of Narita City – Heisei 24 (2012)"]</ref>
== Major Bridges == * '''Kawazuba Bridge''' – [[:en:Japan National Route 296|Route 296]] * '''Koike Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 45|Route 45]] * '''Meiji Bridge''' * '''Matsuba Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 116|Route 116]] * '''Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway (Ken-Ō Expressway)''' * '''Kowa Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 112|Route 112]] * '''Kowa 1st Bridge''' * '''Kowa 5th Bridge''' * '''Kowa 4th Bridge''' * '''Kanao 1st Bridge''' * '''Kanao 2nd Bridge''' * '''Benten Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 22|Route 22]] * '''Kase Bridge''' * '''Ōkura Bridge''' * '''Takoebashi Bridge''' * '''Shin-Takoebashi Bridge''' – [[:en:Japan National Route 126|Route 126]] * '''JR Sōbu Main Line Bridge''' * '''Rokumanbu Bridge''' * '''Nakadai Bridge''' * '''Sakae Bridge''' * '''Komatsu Bridge''' * '''Orito Bridge''' * '''Kido Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 122|Route 122]] * '''Ryokai Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 30|Route 30]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of secondary rivers in Chiba Prefecture]]

The '''first phase''' (non-land acquisition areas) was completed in '''December 2012''', while the remaining sections were scheduled for Completion tier 6]][[Category:by '''March 2014'''.<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 23 (2011), 3rd Session, Sept 6]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 24 (2012), 3rd Session, Sept 11]</ref>

=== Environmental Preservation === The '''"Protect the Beautiful Kido River" Committee''' was founded in '''May 1992''', engaging in '''awareness campaigns and river clean-up efforts'''.<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf "Environmental Report of Narita City – Heisei 24 (2012)"]</ref>
== Major Bridges == * '''Kawazuba Bridge''' – [[:en:Japan National Route 296|Route 296]] * '''Koike Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 45|Route 45]] * '''Meiji Bridge''' * '''Matsuba Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 116|Route 116]] * '''Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway (Ken-Ō Expressway)''' * '''Kowa Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 112|Route 112]] * '''Kowa 1st Bridge''' * '''Kowa 5th Bridge''' * '''Kowa 4th Bridge''' * '''Kanao 1st Bridge''' * '''Kanao 2nd Bridge''' * '''Benten Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 22|Route 22]] * '''Kase Bridge''' * '''Ōkura Bridge''' * '''Takoebashi Bridge''' * '''Shin-Takoebashi Bridge''' – [[:en:Japan National Route 126|Route 126]] * '''JR Sōbu Main Line Bridge''' * '''Rokumanbu Bridge''' * '''Nakadai Bridge''' * '''Sakae Bridge''' * '''Komatsu Bridge''' * '''Orito Bridge''' * '''Kido Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 122|Route 122]] * '''Ryokai Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 30|Route 30]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of secondary rivers in Chiba Prefecture]]

revision.comment: Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:木戸川 (千葉県)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11518415]] Wikidata name: Kido River [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:18, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|Kido River in the Kujūkuri region|the tributary of the Tonegawa system in Funabashi City|Kido River (Funabashi)}} {{Infobox river | name = Kido River | image = [[File:Kidoriver2.jpg|300px]] | image_caption = View downstream from Kido Bridge | watercourse = [[Secondary watercourse (Japan)|Secondary watercourse]] | watercourse_system = Kido River system | type = [[Secondary river]] | length = 21.5 km | elevation = 35 m | discharge = | observation_station = | basin_area = 72.1 km² | source = [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]], Honjō area | mouth = [[:en:Pacific Ocean|Pacific Ocean]] (Sanmu City) | basin = [[Sanmu, Chiba|Sanmu City]], [[Sanbu District, Chiba|Sanbu District]] (Shibayama Town, Yokoshibahikari Town), [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]] | footnotes = | source_url = [https://www.maff.go.jp/kanto/nouson/sekkei/kokuei/ryoso/shizen/01_2.html] }} [[File:Kidoriver1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Upstream view from Kido Bridge]] '''Kido River''' (木戸川, *Kido-gawa*) is a [[secondary river]] flowing through the '''central Kujūkuri Plain''' in '''northeastern Chiba Prefecture, Japan'''. It is the main river of the '''Kido River system'''.
== Geography == The Kido River originates in '''Narita City’s Honjō area''', where it is fed by an '''adjustment pond''', before flowing '''southeast'''. It runs along the '''border of Shibayama Town''' in Sanbu District and then '''passes through the western part of the town''', where it is joined by '''several small tributaries''' from the west.
Exiting Shibayama, the river marks the '''boundary between Yokoshibahikari Town and Sanmu City''' before entering '''Sanmu City'''. Along its course, '''historical sites''' such as the '''Shibayama Kofun Group''' and the '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' can be found. The river then '''exits the valley at Shibahara''', flowing into the '''Kujūkuri Plain''', where it meanders through '''rice fields'''.
Both banks of the river feature '''high ridges''', supporting '''thriving aquatic plant life'''. After passing '''Ryokai Bridge''', the river '''empties into the Pacific Ocean''', bordering '''Kujūkuri Beach'''. The '''Shirahata Beach (west side)''' and '''Minamihama Beach (east side)''' are separated by the river’s mouth.
The river is '''21.5 km long''', with '''20.9 km (from Makino, Shibayama Town, to the mouth) designated as a secondary river'''.
=== Naming === Originally, the Kido River had '''no fixed name'''. Different localities referred to the river by '''various names''': * '''Takoe River''' (*Takoegawa*) in '''Takoe Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Karumo Arai River''' (*Karumo Shingawa*) in '''Karumo Hongō Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Kido River''' (*Kido-gawa*) in '''Kido Village''' (now '''Sanmu City''')
Among these, '''"Kido River"''' became the widely accepted name.
== Flood Control == In '''1966 (Shōwa 41)''', '''a large-scale river improvement project''' was initiated to '''expand and fortify''' the river. The initial phase '''(1966–1972)''' focused on '''4 km from the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge to Komatsu Bridge'''. Later, improvements were made to the '''middle and lower sections''', with the project being completed in '''1997 (Heisei 9)'''.
After Completion tier 6]][[Category:, further work '''remained paused''' except for a '''levee heightening project''' in '''2009''' near the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge.
=== 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake & Tsunami Impact === During the '''2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami''', the Kido River '''sustained significant damage''', surpassing '''all other nearby rivers''' in terms of destruction. The '''tsunami from Iinoura Coast breached the sea wall''' and traveled '''8 km upstream''', reaching '''Takoebashi Bridge'''.<ref>[http://wwwp.pref.chiba.lg.jp/pbgikai/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest06!!25275!1!1!318,-1,318!1986!159440!318,-1,318!1986!159440!324,323,322!14!23!144606!65!1?Template=DocOneFrame Chiba Prefecture Assembly Records – Heisei 24 (2012), 9th Session]</ref>
The '''tsunami destroyed levees in five locations near the river mouth''' and caused '''bank collapses in the middle reaches'''. This '''exposed weaknesses in the flood control system''', leading to a '''1-meter levee reinforcement project''' starting in '''July 2011'''. The '''first phase''' (non-land acquisition areas) was completed in '''December 2012''', while the remaining sections were scheduled for Completion tier 6]][[Category:by '''March 2014'''.<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 23 (2011), 3rd Session, Sept 6]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 24 (2012), 3rd Session, Sept 11]</ref>
A '''proposal for using nearby vacant land as evacuation routes''' was also introduced. Meanwhile, the '''upper course levee reinforcement project''' has continued beyond 2009.
=== Environmental Preservation === The '''"Protect the Beautiful Kido River" Committee''' was founded in '''May 1992''', engaging in '''awareness campaigns and river clean-up efforts'''.<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf "Environmental Report of Narita City – Heisei 24 (2012)"]</ref>
== Major Bridges == * '''Kawazuba Bridge''' – [[:en:Japan National Route 296|Route 296]] * '''Koike Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 45|Route 45]] * '''Meiji Bridge''' * '''Matsuba Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 116|Route 116]] * '''Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway (Ken-Ō Expressway)''' * '''Kowa Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 112|Route 112]] * '''Kowa 1st Bridge''' * '''Kowa 5th Bridge''' * '''Kowa 4th Bridge''' * '''Kanao 1st Bridge''' * '''Kanao 2nd Bridge''' * '''Benten Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 22|Route 22]] * '''Kase Bridge''' * '''Ōkura Bridge''' * '''Takoebashi Bridge''' * '''Shin-Takoebashi Bridge''' – [[:en:Japan National Route 126|Route 126]] * '''JR Sōbu Main Line Bridge''' * '''Rokumanbu Bridge''' * '''Nakadai Bridge''' * '''Sakae Bridge''' * '''Komatsu Bridge''' * '''Orito Bridge''' * '''Kido Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 122|Route 122]] * '''Ryokai Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 30|Route 30]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of secondary rivers in Chiba Prefecture]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:木戸川 (千葉県)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11518415]] Wikidata name: Kido River [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:18, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|Kido River in the Kujūkuri region|the tributary of the Tonegawa system in Funabashi City|Kido River (Funabashi)}} {{Infobox river | name = Kido River | image = [[File:Kidoriver2.jpg|300px]] | image_caption = View downstream from Kido Bridge | watercourse = [[Secondary watercourse (Japan)|Secondary watercourse]] | watercourse_system = Kido River system | type = [[Secondary river]] | length = 21.5 km | elevation = 35 m | discharge = | observation_station = | basin_area = 72.1 km² | source = [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]], Honjō area | mouth = [[:en:Pacific Ocean|Pacific Ocean]] (Sanmu City) | basin = [[Sanmu, Chiba|Sanmu City]], [[Sanbu District, Chiba|Sanbu District]] (Shibayama Town, Yokoshibahikari Town), [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]] | footnotes = | source_url = [https://www.maff.go.jp/kanto/nouson/sekkei/kokuei/ryoso/shizen/01_2.html] }} [[File:Kidoriver1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Upstream view from Kido Bridge]] '''Kido River''' (木戸川, *Kido-gawa*) is a [[secondary river]] flowing through the '''central Kujūkuri Plain''' in '''northeastern Chiba Prefecture, Japan'''. It is the main river of the '''Kido River system'''.
== Geography == The Kido River originates in '''Narita City’s Honjō area''', where it is fed by an '''adjustment pond''', before flowing '''southeast'''. It runs along the '''border of Shibayama Town''' in Sanbu District and then '''passes through the western part of the town''', where it is joined by '''several small tributaries''' from the west.
Exiting Shibayama, the river marks the '''boundary between Yokoshibahikari Town and Sanmu City''' before entering '''Sanmu City'''. Along its course, '''historical sites''' such as the '''Shibayama Kofun Group''' and the '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' can be found. The river then '''exits the valley at Shibahara''', flowing into the '''Kujūkuri Plain''', where it meanders through '''rice fields'''.
Both banks of the river feature '''high ridges''', supporting '''thriving aquatic plant life'''. After passing '''Ryokai Bridge''', the river '''empties into the Pacific Ocean''', bordering '''Kujūkuri Beach'''. The '''Shirahata Beach (west side)''' and '''Minamihama Beach (east side)''' are separated by the river’s mouth.
The river is '''21.5 km long''', with '''20.9 km (from Makino, Shibayama Town, to the mouth) designated as a secondary river'''.
=== Naming === Originally, the Kido River had '''no fixed name'''. Different localities referred to the river by '''various names''': * '''Takoe River''' (*Takoegawa*) in '''Takoe Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Karumo Arai River''' (*Karumo Shingawa*) in '''Karumo Hongō Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Kido River''' (*Kido-gawa*) in '''Kido Village''' (now '''Sanmu City''')
Among these, '''"Kido River"''' became the widely accepted name.
== Flood Control == In '''1966 (Shōwa 41)''', '''a large-scale river improvement project''' was initiated to '''expand and fortify''' the river. The initial phase '''(1966–1972)''' focused on '''4 km from the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge to Komatsu Bridge'''. Later, improvements were made to the '''middle and lower sections''', with the project being completed in '''1997 (Heisei 9)'''.
After Completion tier 6]][[Category:, further work '''remained paused''' except for a '''levee heightening project''' in '''2009''' near the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge.
=== 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake & Tsunami Impact === During the '''2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami''', the Kido River '''sustained significant damage''', surpassing '''all other nearby rivers''' in terms of destruction. The '''tsunami from Iinoura Coast breached the sea wall''' and traveled '''8 km upstream''', reaching '''Takoebashi Bridge'''.<ref>[http://wwwp.pref.chiba.lg.jp/pbgikai/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest06!!25275!1!1!318,-1,318!1986!159440!318,-1,318!1986!159440!324,323,322!14!23!144606!65!1?Template=DocOneFrame Chiba Prefecture Assembly Records – Heisei 24 (2012), 9th Session]</ref>
The '''tsunami destroyed levees in five locations near the river mouth''' and caused '''bank collapses in the middle reaches'''. This '''exposed weaknesses in the flood control system''', leading to a '''1-meter levee reinforcement project''' starting in '''July 2011'''. The '''first phase''' (non-land acquisition areas) was completed in '''December 2012''', while the remaining sections were scheduled for Completion tier 6]][[Category:by '''March 2014'''.<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 23 (2011), 3rd Session, Sept 6]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 24 (2012), 3rd Session, Sept 11]</ref>
A '''proposal for using nearby vacant land as evacuation routes''' was also introduced. Meanwhile, the '''upper course levee reinforcement project''' has continued beyond 2009.
=== Environmental Preservation === The '''"Protect the Beautiful Kido River" Committee''' was founded in '''May 1992''', engaging in '''awareness campaigns and river clean-up efforts'''.<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf "Environmental Report of Narita City – Heisei 24 (2012)"]</ref>
== Major Bridges == * '''Kawazuba Bridge''' – [[:en:Japan National Route 296|Route 296]] * '''Koike Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 45|Route 45]] * '''Meiji Bridge''' * '''Matsuba Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 116|Route 116]] * '''Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway (Ken-Ō Expressway)''' * '''Kowa Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 112|Route 112]] * '''Kowa 1st Bridge''' * '''Kowa 5th Bridge''' * '''Kowa 4th Bridge''' * '''Kanao 1st Bridge''' * '''Kanao 2nd Bridge''' * '''Benten Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 22|Route 22]] * '''Kase Bridge''' * '''Ōkura Bridge''' * '''Takoebashi Bridge''' * '''Shin-Takoebashi Bridge''' – [[:en:Japan National Route 126|Route 126]] * '''JR Sōbu Main Line Bridge''' * '''Rokumanbu Bridge''' * '''Nakadai Bridge''' * '''Sakae Bridge''' * '''Komatsu Bridge''' * '''Orito Bridge''' * '''Kido Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 122|Route 122]] * '''Ryokai Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 30|Route 30]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of secondary rivers in Chiba Prefecture]]

The river is '''21.5 km long''', with '''20.9 km (from Makino, Shibayama Town, to the mouth) designated as a secondary river'''.
=== Naming === Originally, the Kido River had '''no fixed name'''. Different localities referred to the river by '''various names''': * '''Takoe River''' (*Takoegawa*) in '''Takoe Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Karumo Arai River''' (*Karumo Shingawa*) in '''Karumo Hongō Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Kido River''' (*Kido-gawa*) in '''Kido Village''' (now '''Sanmu City''')
Among these, '''"Kido River"''' became the widely accepted name.
== Flood Control == In '''1966 (Shōwa 41)''', '''a large-scale river improvement project''' was initiated to '''expand and fortify''' the river. The initial phase '''(1966–1972)''' focused on '''4 km from the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge to Komatsu Bridge'''. Later, improvements were made to the '''middle and lower sections''', with the project being completed in '''1997 (Heisei 9)'''.
After Completion tier 6]][[Category:, further work '''remained paused''' except for a '''levee heightening project''' in '''2009''' near the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge.
=== 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake & Tsunami Impact === During the '''2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami''', the Kido River '''sustained significant damage''', surpassing '''all other nearby rivers''' in terms of destruction. The '''tsunami from Iinoura Coast breached the sea wall''' and traveled '''8 km upstream''', reaching '''Takoebashi Bridge'''.<ref>[http://wwwp.pref.chiba.lg.jp/pbgikai/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest06!!25275!1!1!318,-1,318!1986!159440!318,-1,318!1986!159440!324,323,322!14!23!144606!65!1?Template=DocOneFrame Chiba Prefecture Assembly Records – Heisei 24 (2012), 9th Session]</ref>
The '''tsunami destroyed levees in five locations near the river mouth''' and caused '''bank collapses in the middle reaches'''. This '''exposed weaknesses in the flood control system''', leading to a '''1-meter levee reinforcement project''' starting in '''July 2011'''. The '''first phase''' (non-land acquisition areas) was completed in '''December 2012''', while the remaining sections were scheduled for Completion tier 6]][[Category:by '''March 2014'''.<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 23 (2011), 3rd Session, Sept 6]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 24 (2012), 3rd Session, Sept 11]</ref>
A '''proposal for using nearby vacant land as evacuation routes''' was also introduced. Meanwhile, the '''upper course levee reinforcement project''' has continued beyond 2009.
=== Environmental Preservation === The '''"Protect the Beautiful Kido River" Committee''' was founded in '''May 1992''', engaging in '''awareness campaigns and river clean-up efforts'''.<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf "Environmental Report of Narita City – Heisei 24 (2012)"]</ref>
== Major Bridges == * '''Kawazuba Bridge''' – [[:en:Japan National Route 296|Route 296]] * '''Koike Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 45|Route 45]] * '''Meiji Bridge''' * '''Matsuba Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 116|Route 116]] * '''Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway (Ken-Ō Expressway)''' * '''Kowa Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 112|Route 112]] * '''Kowa 1st Bridge''' * '''Kowa 5th Bridge''' * '''Kowa 4th Bridge''' * '''Kanao 1st Bridge''' * '''Kanao 2nd Bridge''' * '''Benten Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 22|Route 22]] * '''Kase Bridge''' * '''Ōkura Bridge''' * '''Takoebashi Bridge''' * '''Shin-Takoebashi Bridge''' – [[:en:Japan National Route 126|Route 126]] * '''JR Sōbu Main Line Bridge''' * '''Rokumanbu Bridge''' * '''Nakadai Bridge''' * '''Sakae Bridge''' * '''Komatsu Bridge''' * '''Orito Bridge''' * '''Kido Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 122|Route 122]] * '''Ryokai Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 30|Route 30]]
== References == {{Reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:木戸川 (千葉県)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11518415]] Wikidata name: Kido River [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:18, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|Kido River in the Kujūkuri region|the tributary of the Tonegawa system in Funabashi City|Kido River (Funabashi)}} {{Infobox river | name = Kido River | image = [[File:Kidoriver2.jpg|300px]] | image_caption = View downstream from Kido Bridge | watercourse = [[Secondary watercourse (Japan)|Secondary watercourse]] | watercourse_system = Kido River system | type = [[Secondary river]] | length = 21.5 km | elevation = 35 m | discharge = | observation_station = | basin_area = 72.1 km² | source = [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]], Honjō area | mouth = [[:en:Pacific Ocean|Pacific Ocean]] (Sanmu City) | basin = [[Sanmu, Chiba|Sanmu City]], [[Sanbu District, Chiba|Sanbu District]] (Shibayama Town, Yokoshibahikari Town), [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]] | footnotes = | source_url = [https://www.maff.go.jp/kanto/nouson/sekkei/kokuei/ryoso/shizen/01_2.html] }} [[File:Kidoriver1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Upstream view from Kido Bridge]] '''Kido River''' (木戸川, *Kido-gawa*) is a [[secondary river]] flowing through the '''central Kujūkuri Plain''' in '''northeastern Chiba Prefecture, Japan'''. It is the main river of the '''Kido River system'''.
== Geography == The Kido River originates in '''Narita City’s Honjō area''', where it is fed by an '''adjustment pond''', before flowing '''southeast'''. It runs along the '''border of Shibayama Town''' in Sanbu District and then '''passes through the western part of the town''', where it is joined by '''several small tributaries''' from the west.
Exiting Shibayama, the river marks the '''boundary between Yokoshibahikari Town and Sanmu City''' before entering '''Sanmu City'''. Along its course, '''historical sites''' such as the '''Shibayama Kofun Group''' and the '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' can be found. The river then '''exits the valley at Shibahara''', flowing into the '''Kujūkuri Plain''', where it meanders through '''rice fields'''.
Both banks of the river feature '''high ridges''', supporting '''thriving aquatic plant life'''. After passing '''Ryokai Bridge''', the river '''empties into the Pacific Ocean''', bordering '''Kujūkuri Beach'''. The '''Shirahata Beach (west side)''' and '''Minamihama Beach (east side)''' are separated by the river’s mouth.
The river is '''21.5 km long''', with '''20.9 km (from Makino, Shibayama Town, to the mouth) designated as a secondary river'''.
=== Naming === Originally, the Kido River had '''no fixed name'''. Different localities referred to the river by '''various names''': * '''Takoe River''' (*Takoegawa*) in '''Takoe Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Karumo Arai River''' (*Karumo Shingawa*) in '''Karumo Hongō Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Kido River''' (*Kido-gawa*) in '''Kido Village''' (now '''Sanmu City''')
Among these, '''"Kido River"''' became the widely accepted name.
== Flood Control == In '''1966 (Shōwa 41)''', '''a large-scale river improvement project''' was initiated to '''expand and fortify''' the river. The initial phase '''(1966–1972)''' focused on '''4 km from the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge to Komatsu Bridge'''. Later, improvements were made to the '''middle and lower sections''', with the project being completed in '''1997 (Heisei 9)'''.
After Completion tier 6]][[Category:, further work '''remained paused''' except for a '''levee heightening project''' in '''2009''' near the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge.
=== 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake & Tsunami Impact === During the '''2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami''', the Kido River '''sustained significant damage''', surpassing '''all other nearby rivers''' in terms of destruction. The '''tsunami from Iinoura Coast breached the sea wall''' and traveled '''8 km upstream''', reaching '''Takoebashi Bridge'''.<ref>[http://wwwp.pref.chiba.lg.jp/pbgikai/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest06!!25275!1!1!318,-1,318!1986!159440!318,-1,318!1986!159440!324,323,322!14!23!144606!65!1?Template=DocOneFrame Chiba Prefecture Assembly Records – Heisei 24 (2012), 9th Session]</ref>
The '''tsunami destroyed levees in five locations near the river mouth''' and caused '''bank collapses in the middle reaches'''. This '''exposed weaknesses in the flood control system''', leading to a '''1-meter levee reinforcement project''' starting in '''July 2011'''. The '''first phase''' (non-land acquisition areas) was completed in '''December 2012''', while the remaining sections were scheduled for Completion tier 6]][[Category:by '''March 2014'''.<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 23 (2011), 3rd Session, Sept 6]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 24 (2012), 3rd Session, Sept 11]</ref>
A '''proposal for using nearby vacant land as evacuation routes''' was also introduced. Meanwhile, the '''upper course levee reinforcement project''' has continued beyond 2009.
=== Environmental Preservation === The '''"Protect the Beautiful Kido River" Committee''' was founded in '''May 1992''', engaging in '''awareness campaigns and river clean-up efforts'''.<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf "Environmental Report of Narita City – Heisei 24 (2012)"]</ref>
== Major Bridges == * '''Kawazuba Bridge''' – [[:en:Japan National Route 296|Route 296]] * '''Koike Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 45|Route 45]] * '''Meiji Bridge''' * '''Matsuba Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 116|Route 116]] * '''Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway (Ken-Ō Expressway)''' * '''Kowa Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 112|Route 112]] * '''Kowa 1st Bridge''' * '''Kowa 5th Bridge''' * '''Kowa 4th Bridge''' * '''Kanao 1st Bridge''' * '''Kanao 2nd Bridge''' * '''Benten Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 22|Route 22]] * '''Kase Bridge''' * '''Ōkura Bridge''' * '''Takoebashi Bridge''' * '''Shin-Takoebashi Bridge''' – [[:en:Japan National Route 126|Route 126]] * '''JR Sōbu Main Line Bridge''' * '''Rokumanbu Bridge''' * '''Nakadai Bridge''' * '''Sakae Bridge''' * '''Komatsu Bridge''' * '''Orito Bridge''' * '''Kido Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 122|Route 122]] * '''Ryokai Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 30|Route 30]]

A '''proposal for using nearby vacant land as evacuation routes''' was also introduced. Meanwhile, the '''upper course levee reinforcement project''' has continued beyond 2009.
=== Environmental Preservation === The '''"Protect the Beautiful Kido River" Committee''' was founded in '''May 1992''', engaging in '''awareness campaigns and river clean-up efforts'''.<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf "Environmental Report of Narita City – Heisei 24 (2012)"]</ref>
== Major Bridges == * '''Kawazuba Bridge''' – [[:en:Japan National Route 296|Route 296]] * '''Koike Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 45|Route 45]] * '''Meiji Bridge''' * '''Matsuba Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 116|Route 116]] * '''Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway (Ken-Ō Expressway)''' * '''Kowa Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 112|Route 112]] * '''Kowa 1st Bridge''' * '''Kowa 5th Bridge''' * '''Kowa 4th Bridge''' * '''Kanao 1st Bridge''' * '''Kanao 2nd Bridge''' * '''Benten Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 22|Route 22]] * '''Kase Bridge''' * '''Ōkura Bridge''' * '''Takoebashi Bridge''' * '''Shin-Takoebashi Bridge''' – [[:en:Japan National Route 126|Route 126]] * '''JR Sōbu Main Line Bridge''' * '''Rokumanbu Bridge''' * '''Nakadai Bridge''' * '''Sakae Bridge''' * '''Komatsu Bridge''' * '''Orito Bridge''' * '''Kido Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 122|Route 122]] * '''Ryokai Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 30|Route 30]]

revision.comment: Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:木戸川 (千葉県)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:木戸川 (千葉県)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:木戸川 (千葉県)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11518415]] Wikidata name: Kido River [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:18, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|Kido River in the Kujūkuri region|the tributary of the Tonegawa system in Funabashi City|Kido River (Funabashi)}} {{Infobox river | name = Kido River | image = [[File:Kidoriver2.jpg|300px]] | image_caption = View downstream from Kido Bridge | watercourse = [[Secondary watercourse (Japan)|Secondary watercourse]] | watercourse_system = Kido River system | type = [[Secondary river]] | length = 21.5 km | elevation = 35 m | discharge = | observation_station = | basin_area = 72.1 km² | source = [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]], Honjō area | mouth = [[:en:Pacific Ocean|Pacific Ocean]] (Sanmu City) | basin = [[Sanmu, Chiba|Sanmu City]], [[Sanbu District, Chiba|Sanbu District]] (Shibayama Town, Yokoshibahikari Town), [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]] | footnotes = | source_url = [https://www.maff.go.jp/kanto/nouson/sekkei/kokuei/ryoso/shizen/01_2.html] }} [[File:Kidoriver1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Upstream view from Kido Bridge]] '''Kido River''' (木戸川, *Kido-gawa*) is a [[secondary river]] flowing through the '''central Kujūkuri Plain''' in '''northeastern Chiba Prefecture, Japan'''. It is the main river of the '''Kido River system'''.
== Geography == The Kido River originates in '''Narita City’s Honjō area''', where it is fed by an '''adjustment pond''', before flowing '''southeast'''. It runs along the '''border of Shibayama Town''' in Sanbu District and then '''passes through the western part of the town''', where it is joined by '''several small tributaries''' from the west.
Exiting Shibayama, the river marks the '''boundary between Yokoshibahikari Town and Sanmu City''' before entering '''Sanmu City'''. Along its course, '''historical sites''' such as the '''Shibayama Kofun Group''' and the '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' can be found. The river then '''exits the valley at Shibahara''', flowing into the '''Kujūkuri Plain''', where it meanders through '''rice fields'''.
Both banks of the river feature '''high ridges''', supporting '''thriving aquatic plant life'''. After passing '''Ryokai Bridge''', the river '''empties into the Pacific Ocean''', bordering '''Kujūkuri Beach'''. The '''Shirahata Beach (west side)''' and '''Minamihama Beach (east side)''' are separated by the river’s mouth.
The river is '''21.5 km long''', with '''20.9 km (from Makino, Shibayama Town, to the mouth) designated as a secondary river'''.
=== Naming === Originally, the Kido River had '''no fixed name'''. Different localities referred to the river by '''various names''': * '''Takoe River''' (*Takoegawa*) in '''Takoe Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Karumo Arai River''' (*Karumo Shingawa*) in '''Karumo Hongō Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Kido River''' (*Kido-gawa*) in '''Kido Village''' (now '''Sanmu City''')
Among these, '''"Kido River"''' became the widely accepted name.
== Flood Control == In '''1966 (Shōwa 41)''', '''a large-scale river improvement project''' was initiated to '''expand and fortify''' the river. The initial phase '''(1966–1972)''' focused on '''4 km from the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge to Komatsu Bridge'''. Later, improvements were made to the '''middle and lower sections''', with the project being completed in '''1997 (Heisei 9)'''.
After Completion tier 6]][[Category:, further work '''remained paused''' except for a '''levee heightening project''' in '''2009''' near the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge.
=== 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake & Tsunami Impact === During the '''2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami''', the Kido River '''sustained significant damage''', surpassing '''all other nearby rivers''' in terms of destruction. The '''tsunami from Iinoura Coast breached the sea wall''' and traveled '''8 km upstream''', reaching '''Takoebashi Bridge'''.<ref>[http://wwwp.pref.chiba.lg.jp/pbgikai/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest06!!25275!1!1!318,-1,318!1986!159440!318,-1,318!1986!159440!324,323,322!14!23!144606!65!1?Template=DocOneFrame Chiba Prefecture Assembly Records – Heisei 24 (2012), 9th Session]</ref>
The '''tsunami destroyed levees in five locations near the river mouth''' and caused '''bank collapses in the middle reaches'''. This '''exposed weaknesses in the flood control system''', leading to a '''1-meter levee reinforcement project''' starting in '''July 2011'''. The '''first phase''' (non-land acquisition areas) was completed in '''December 2012''', while the remaining sections were scheduled for Completion tier 6]][[Category:by '''March 2014'''.<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 23 (2011), 3rd Session, Sept 6]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 24 (2012), 3rd Session, Sept 11]</ref>
A '''proposal for using nearby vacant land as evacuation routes''' was also introduced. Meanwhile, the '''upper course levee reinforcement project''' has continued beyond 2009.
=== Environmental Preservation === The '''"Protect the Beautiful Kido River" Committee''' was founded in '''May 1992''', engaging in '''awareness campaigns and river clean-up efforts'''.<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf "Environmental Report of Narita City – Heisei 24 (2012)"]</ref>
== Major Bridges == * '''Kawazuba Bridge''' – [[:en:Japan National Route 296|Route 296]] * '''Koike Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 45|Route 45]] * '''Meiji Bridge''' * '''Matsuba Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 116|Route 116]] * '''Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway (Ken-Ō Expressway)''' * '''Kowa Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 112|Route 112]] * '''Kowa 1st Bridge''' * '''Kowa 5th Bridge''' * '''Kowa 4th Bridge''' * '''Kanao 1st Bridge''' * '''Kanao 2nd Bridge''' * '''Benten Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 22|Route 22]] * '''Kase Bridge''' * '''Ōkura Bridge''' * '''Takoebashi Bridge''' * '''Shin-Takoebashi Bridge''' – [[:en:Japan National Route 126|Route 126]] * '''JR Sōbu Main Line Bridge''' * '''Rokumanbu Bridge''' * '''Nakadai Bridge''' * '''Sakae Bridge''' * '''Komatsu Bridge''' * '''Orito Bridge''' * '''Kido Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 122|Route 122]] * '''Ryokai Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 30|Route 30]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of secondary rivers in Chiba Prefecture]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:木戸川 (千葉県)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:木戸川 (千葉県)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:木戸川 (千葉県)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11518415]] Wikidata name: Kido River [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:18, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|Kido River in the Kujūkuri region|the tributary of the Tonegawa system in Funabashi City|Kido River (Funabashi)}} {{Infobox river | name = Kido River | image = [[File:Kidoriver2.jpg|300px]] | image_caption = View downstream from Kido Bridge | watercourse = [[Secondary watercourse (Japan)|Secondary watercourse]] | watercourse_system = Kido River system | type = [[Secondary river]] | length = 21.5 km | elevation = 35 m | discharge = | observation_station = | basin_area = 72.1 km² | source = [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]], Honjō area | mouth = [[:en:Pacific Ocean|Pacific Ocean]] (Sanmu City) | basin = [[Sanmu, Chiba|Sanmu City]], [[Sanbu District, Chiba|Sanbu District]] (Shibayama Town, Yokoshibahikari Town), [[Narita, Chiba|Narita City]] | footnotes = | source_url = [https://www.maff.go.jp/kanto/nouson/sekkei/kokuei/ryoso/shizen/01_2.html] }} [[File:Kidoriver1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Upstream view from Kido Bridge]] '''Kido River''' (木戸川, *Kido-gawa*) is a [[secondary river]] flowing through the '''central Kujūkuri Plain''' in '''northeastern Chiba Prefecture, Japan'''. It is the main river of the '''Kido River system'''.
== Geography == The Kido River originates in '''Narita City’s Honjō area''', where it is fed by an '''adjustment pond''', before flowing '''southeast'''. It runs along the '''border of Shibayama Town''' in Sanbu District and then '''passes through the western part of the town''', where it is joined by '''several small tributaries''' from the west.
Exiting Shibayama, the river marks the '''boundary between Yokoshibahikari Town and Sanmu City''' before entering '''Sanmu City'''. Along its course, '''historical sites''' such as the '''Shibayama Kofun Group''' and the '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' can be found. The river then '''exits the valley at Shibahara''', flowing into the '''Kujūkuri Plain''', where it meanders through '''rice fields'''.
Both banks of the river feature '''high ridges''', supporting '''thriving aquatic plant life'''. After passing '''Ryokai Bridge''', the river '''empties into the Pacific Ocean''', bordering '''Kujūkuri Beach'''. The '''Shirahata Beach (west side)''' and '''Minamihama Beach (east side)''' are separated by the river’s mouth.
The river is '''21.5 km long''', with '''20.9 km (from Makino, Shibayama Town, to the mouth) designated as a secondary river'''.
=== Naming === Originally, the Kido River had '''no fixed name'''. Different localities referred to the river by '''various names''': * '''Takoe River''' (*Takoegawa*) in '''Takoe Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Karumo Arai River''' (*Karumo Shingawa*) in '''Karumo Hongō Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Kido River''' (*Kido-gawa*) in '''Kido Village''' (now '''Sanmu City''')
Among these, '''"Kido River"''' became the widely accepted name.
== Flood Control == In '''1966 (Shōwa 41)''', '''a large-scale river improvement project''' was initiated to '''expand and fortify''' the river. The initial phase '''(1966–1972)''' focused on '''4 km from the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge to Komatsu Bridge'''. Later, improvements were made to the '''middle and lower sections''', with the project being completed in '''1997 (Heisei 9)'''.
After Completion tier 6]][[Category:, further work '''remained paused''' except for a '''levee heightening project''' in '''2009''' near the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge.
=== 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake & Tsunami Impact === During the '''2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami''', the Kido River '''sustained significant damage''', surpassing '''all other nearby rivers''' in terms of destruction. The '''tsunami from Iinoura Coast breached the sea wall''' and traveled '''8 km upstream''', reaching '''Takoebashi Bridge'''.<ref>[http://wwwp.pref.chiba.lg.jp/pbgikai/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest06!!25275!1!1!318,-1,318!1986!159440!318,-1,318!1986!159440!324,323,322!14!23!144606!65!1?Template=DocOneFrame Chiba Prefecture Assembly Records – Heisei 24 (2012), 9th Session]</ref>
The '''tsunami destroyed levees in five locations near the river mouth''' and caused '''bank collapses in the middle reaches'''. This '''exposed weaknesses in the flood control system''', leading to a '''1-meter levee reinforcement project''' starting in '''July 2011'''. The '''first phase''' (non-land acquisition areas) was completed in '''December 2012''', while the remaining sections were scheduled for Completion tier 6]][[Category:by '''March 2014'''.<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 23 (2011), 3rd Session, Sept 6]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 24 (2012), 3rd Session, Sept 11]</ref>
A '''proposal for using nearby vacant land as evacuation routes''' was also introduced. Meanwhile, the '''upper course levee reinforcement project''' has continued beyond 2009.
=== Environmental Preservation === The '''"Protect the Beautiful Kido River" Committee''' was founded in '''May 1992''', engaging in '''awareness campaigns and river clean-up efforts'''.<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf "Environmental Report of Narita City – Heisei 24 (2012)"]</ref>
== Major Bridges == * '''Kawazuba Bridge''' – [[:en:Japan National Route 296|Route 296]] * '''Koike Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 45|Route 45]] * '''Meiji Bridge''' * '''Matsuba Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 116|Route 116]] * '''Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway (Ken-Ō Expressway)''' * '''Kowa Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 112|Route 112]] * '''Kowa 1st Bridge''' * '''Kowa 5th Bridge''' * '''Kowa 4th Bridge''' * '''Kanao 1st Bridge''' * '''Kanao 2nd Bridge''' * '''Benten Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 22|Route 22]] * '''Kase Bridge''' * '''Ōkura Bridge''' * '''Takoebashi Bridge''' * '''Shin-Takoebashi Bridge''' – [[:en:Japan National Route 126|Route 126]] * '''JR Sōbu Main Line Bridge''' * '''Rokumanbu Bridge''' * '''Nakadai Bridge''' * '''Sakae Bridge''' * '''Komatsu Bridge''' * '''Orito Bridge''' * '''Kido Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 122|Route 122]] * '''Ryokai Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 30|Route 30]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of secondary rivers in Chiba Prefecture]]
{{translated page|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)|version=102673435|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It runs along the '''border of Shibayama Town''' in Sanbu District and then '''passes through the western part of the town''', where it is joined by '''several small tributaries''' from the west.
Exiting Shibayama, the river marks the '''boundary between Yokoshibahikari Town and Sanmu City''' before entering '''Sanmu City'''. Along its course, '''historical sites''' such as the '''Shibayama Kofun Group''' and the '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' can be found. The river then '''exits the valley at Shibahara''', flowing into the '''Kujūkuri Plain''', where it meanders through '''rice fields'''.
Both banks of the river feature '''high ridges''', supporting '''thriving aquatic plant life'''. After passing '''Ryokai Bridge''', the river '''empties into the Pacific Ocean''', bordering '''Kujūkuri Beach'''. The '''Shirahata Beach (west side)''' and '''Minamihama Beach (east side)''' are separated by the river’s mouth.
The river is '''21.5 km long''', with '''20.9 km (from Makino, Shibayama Town, to the mouth) designated as a secondary river'''.
=== Naming === Originally, the Kido River had '''no fixed name'''. Different localities referred to the river by '''various names''': * '''Takoe River''' (*Takoegawa*) in '''Takoe Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Karumo Arai River''' (*Karumo Shingawa*) in '''Karumo Hongō Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Kido River''' (*Kido-gawa*) in '''Kido Village''' (now '''Sanmu City''')
Among these, '''"Kido River"''' became the widely accepted name.
== Flood Control == In '''1966 (Shōwa 41)''', '''a large-scale river improvement project''' was initiated to '''expand and fortify''' the river. The initial phase '''(1966–1972)''' focused on '''4 km from the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge to Komatsu Bridge'''. Later, improvements were made to the '''middle and lower sections''', with the project being completed in '''1997 (Heisei 9)'''.
After Completion tier 6]][[Category:, further work '''remained paused''' except for a '''levee heightening project''' in '''2009''' near the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge.
=== 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake & Tsunami Impact === During the '''2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami''', the Kido River '''sustained significant damage''', surpassing '''all other nearby rivers''' in terms of destruction. The '''tsunami from Iinoura Coast breached the sea wall''' and traveled '''8 km upstream''', reaching '''Takoebashi Bridge'''.<ref>[http://wwwp.pref.chiba.lg.jp/pbgikai/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest06!!25275!1!1!318,-1,318!1986!159440!318,-1,318!1986!159440!324,323,322!14!23!144606!65!1?Template=DocOneFrame Chiba Prefecture Assembly Records – Heisei 24 (2012), 9th Session]</ref>
The '''tsunami destroyed levees in five locations near the river mouth''' and caused '''bank collapses in the middle reaches'''. This '''exposed weaknesses in the flood control system''', leading to a '''1-meter levee reinforcement project''' starting in '''July 2011'''. The '''first phase''' (non-land acquisition areas) was completed in '''December 2012''', while the remaining sections were scheduled for Completion tier 6]][[Category:by '''March 2014'''.<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 23 (2011), 3rd Session, Sept 6]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 24 (2012), 3rd Session, Sept 11]</ref>
A '''proposal for using nearby vacant land as evacuation routes''' was also introduced. Meanwhile, the '''upper course levee reinforcement project''' has continued beyond 2009.
=== Environmental Preservation === The '''"Protect the Beautiful Kido River" Committee''' was founded in '''May 1992''', engaging in '''awareness campaigns and river clean-up efforts'''.<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf "Environmental Report of Narita City – Heisei 24 (2012)"]</ref>
== Major Bridges == * '''Kawazuba Bridge''' – {{ill|Japan National Route 296|en|Japan National Route 296|WD=Q11421439|lt=Route 296}} * '''Koike Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 45|Route 45]] * '''Meiji Bridge''' * '''Matsuba Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 116|Route 116]] * '''Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway (Ken-Ō Expressway)''' * '''Kowa Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 112|Route 112]] * '''Kowa 1st Bridge''' * '''Kowa 5th Bridge''' * '''Kowa 4th Bridge''' * '''Kanao 1st Bridge''' * '''Kanao 2nd Bridge''' * '''Benten Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 22|Route 22]] * '''Kase Bridge''' * '''Ōkura Bridge''' * '''Takoebashi Bridge''' * '''Shin-Takoebashi Bridge''' – {{ill|Japan National Route 126|en|Japan National Route 126|WD=Q6156754|lt=Route 126}} * '''JR Sōbu Main Line Bridge''' * '''Rokumanbu Bridge''' * '''Nakadai Bridge''' * '''Sakae Bridge''' * '''Komatsu Bridge''' * '''Orito Bridge''' * '''Kido Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 122|Route 122]] * '''Ryokai Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 30|Route 30]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of secondary rivers in Chiba Prefecture]]

It is the main river of the '''Kido River system'''.

It runs along the '''border of Shibayama Town''' in Sanbu District and then '''passes through the western part of the town''', where it is joined by '''several small tributaries''' from the west.
Exiting Shibayama, the river marks the '''boundary between Yokoshibahikari Town and Sanmu City''' before entering '''Sanmu City'''. Along its course, '''historical sites''' such as the '''Shibayama Kofun Group''' and the '''Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun''' can be found. The river then '''exits the valley at Shibahara''', flowing into the '''Kujūkuri Plain''', where it meanders through '''rice fields'''.

The river is '''21.5 km long''', with '''20.9 km (from Makino, Shibayama Town, to the mouth) designated as a secondary river'''.

Different localities referred to the river by '''various names''': * '''Takoe River''' (*Takoegawa*) in '''Takoe Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Karumo Arai River''' (*Karumo Shingawa*) in '''Karumo Hongō Village''' (now '''Matsuo Town, Sanmu City''') * '''Kido River''' (*Kido-gawa*) in '''Kido Village''' (now '''Sanmu City''')
Among these, '''"Kido River"''' became the widely accepted name.

The initial phase '''(1966–1972)''' focused on '''4 km from the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge to Komatsu Bridge'''. Later, improvements were made to the '''middle and lower sections''', with the project being completed in '''1997 (Heisei 9)'''.
After Completion tier 6]][[Category:, further work '''remained paused''' except for a '''levee heightening project''' in '''2009''' near the JR Sōbu Main Line bridge.

The '''tsunami from Iinoura Coast breached the sea wall''' and traveled '''8 km upstream''', reaching '''Takoebashi Bridge'''.<ref>[http://wwwp.pref.chiba.lg.jp/pbgikai/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest06!!25275!1!1!318,-1,318!1986!159440!318,-1,318!1986!159440!324,323,322!14!23!144606!65!1?Template=DocOneFrame Chiba Prefecture Assembly Records – Heisei 24 (2012), 9th Session]</ref>
The '''tsunami destroyed levees in five locations near the river mouth''' and caused '''bank collapses in the middle reaches'''. This '''exposed weaknesses in the flood control system''', leading to a '''1-meter levee reinforcement project''' starting in '''July 2011'''. The '''first phase''' (non-land acquisition areas) was completed in '''December 2012''', while the remaining sections were scheduled for Completion tier 6]][[Category:by '''March 2014'''.<ref>[http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!18592!197!22?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 23 (2011), 3rd Session, Sept 6]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305110315/http://asp.db-search.com/sammu-c/dsweb.cgi/documentframe!1!guest05!!6390!0!1!1,-1,1!418!27148!1,-1,1!418!27148!4,3,2!31!94!24515!520!26?Template=DocOneFrame Sanmu City Council Minutes – Heisei 24 (2012), 3rd Session, Sept 11]</ref>
A '''proposal for using nearby vacant land as evacuation routes''' was also introduced. Meanwhile, the '''upper course levee reinforcement project''' has continued beyond 2009.
=== Environmental Preservation === The '''"Protect the Beautiful Kido River" Committee''' was founded in '''May 1992''', engaging in '''awareness campaigns and river clean-up efforts'''.<ref>[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/DAT/000044897.pdf "Environmental Report of Narita City – Heisei 24 (2012)"]</ref>
== Major Bridges == * '''Kawazuba Bridge''' – {{ill|Japan National Route 296|en|Japan National Route 296|WD=Q11421439|lt=Route 296}} * '''Koike Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 45|Route 45]] * '''Meiji Bridge''' * '''Matsuba Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 116|Route 116]] * '''Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway (Ken-Ō Expressway)''' * '''Kowa Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 112|Route 112]] * '''Kowa 1st Bridge''' * '''Kowa 5th Bridge''' * '''Kowa 4th Bridge''' * '''Kanao 1st Bridge''' * '''Kanao 2nd Bridge''' * '''Benten Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 22|Route 22]] * '''Kase Bridge''' * '''Ōkura Bridge''' * '''Takoebashi Bridge''' * '''Shin-Takoebashi Bridge''' – {{ill|Japan National Route 126|en|Japan National Route 126|WD=Q6156754|lt=Route 126}} * '''JR Sōbu Main Line Bridge''' * '''Rokumanbu Bridge''' * '''Nakadai Bridge''' * '''Sakae Bridge''' * '''Komatsu Bridge''' * '''Orito Bridge''' * '''Kido Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 122|Route 122]] * '''Ryokai Bridge''' – [[Chiba Prefectural Route 30|Route 30]]
